Acreditamos que grandes pessoas fazem empresas de sucesso e por isso mesmo defendemos a valorização, reconhecimento e crescimento dos nossos profissionais.

Não queremos ser mais uma linha do teu CV, queremos que connosco vivas uma Lifetime Experience. Apostamos em formações e certificações que confirmam a tua real progressão, na promoção de um saudável work-life balance e na oferta de benefícios com impacto na tua vida pessoal e carreira.



O que procuramos

  • Licenciatura em Economia, Estatística, Matemática, Gestão ou similar;
  • Mais de 3 anos de experiência profissional em funções como Data Analyst;
  • Conhecimentos sólidos de SQL Server;
  • Conhecimentos sólidos de PowerBI (dashboards, DAX, modelagem de dados);
  • Conhecimentos sólidos de SSIS
  • Desenvolver, executar e automatizar processos ETL
  • Bons conhecimentos de inglês (B2 ou superior).



Ser Affinity

#Affinidade – Criares relações de proximidade e empatia com colegas, clientes e candidatos (e não apenas com tecnologias, linguagens e plataformas). Espírito de equipa e boa disposição;

· #Ambição – Quereres fazer mais e melhor. Vontade de exceder expectativas e evoluir pessoal e profissionalmente;

· #Ação – Energia para fazer acontecer. Mostrares proatividade e capacidade de iniciativa;

· #Aprendizagem – Vontade de evoluir individual e coletivamente e tornares-te um expert no mercado tecnológico;

· #Assertividade – Comunicação transparente e honesta, sempre com feedback construtivo.



A tua #LifetimeExperience

  • Participar em projetos nacionais e internacionais numa empresa baseada em relacionamentos pessoais, na simplicidade e na eficiência, com uma ação disruptiva no mercado tecnológico;
  • Aceder a um plano de carreira e formação personalizado e diretamente proporcional ao teu desempenho e interesse;
  • Fazer parte de um ambiente acolhedor, de confiança, respeito e informalidade;
  • Integrar as nossas Affinity Communities (de desporto, tecnologia, hobbies, etc.) e participar ativamente em projetos de responsabilidade social e ambiental;
  • Tirar partido de um conjunto de benefícios, parcerias, descontos eventos e dinâmicas internas.


Para além da tua satisfação profissional queremos proporcionar-te oportunidades de lazer e convívio memoráveis e dignos de uma experiência Affinity.

Join the Experience!


Envia-nos a tua candidatura e aproveita para nos conhecer e ir acompanhando em:

https://www.instagram.com/affinityportugal/

https://www.linkedin.com/company/2701281/admin/

  • https://twitter.com/WithAffinity

Title: Internship as Data Analyst
Description:
Stellantis is a leading global mobility player guided by a clear mission: to provide freedom of movement for all through distinctive, appealing, affordable and sustainable mobility solutions. Our Company's strength lies in the breadth of our iconic brand portfolio, the diversity and passion of our 300,000 people, and our deep roots in the communities in which we operate.




Role Overview

Stellantis is looking for a Data Analyst Intern to join the Engineering team, supporting data-driven activities related to product development and engineering processes.

This internship offers the opportunity to work in a dynamic and international environment, contributing to transforming complex data into actionable insights that support both technical and business decisions.




Key Responsibilities

Collect, clean and analyze data from multiple sources (e.g., testing, simulations, production)
Support the development of dashboards and reporting tools for engineering teams
Perform exploratory data analysis to identify trends, anomalies and improvement opportunities
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (engineering, quality, manufacturing)
Contribute to the development of basic analytical models and data visualization tools
Support the automation of reporting and data analysis processes




Requirements

Master’s Degree (in progress) in Engineering, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics or other related fields
Strong interest in the automotive sector and engineering processes
Good knowledge of data analysis tools (e.g., Excel, Python, R or similar)
Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau) is a plus
Strong analytical skills and attention to detail
Good command of Italian and English




Soft Skills

Problem-solving mindset
Teamwork and collaboration
Proactivity and curiosity
Ability to manage priorities




What We Offer

Hands-on experience in an international and innovative environment
Exposure to real-world, high-impact projects
Mentorship from experienced professionals
A dynamic workplace focused on technological innovation







At Stellantis, we assess candidates based on qualifications, merit and business needs. We welcome applications from people of all gender identities, age, ethnicity, nationality, religion, sexual orientation and disability.

Diverse teams, will allow us to better meet the evolving needs of our customers and care for our future.

By submitting your application, you are accepting our privacy notice: https://www.stellantis.com/en/privacy

Title: IT Specialist
Description:
SASMAR is seeking an experienced IT Specialist to join our dynamic team. We are a leading pharmaceutical company that specializes in innovative personal care and fertility products, notably our well-known Conceive Plus brand. Our products are distributed in over 70 countries through various distribution channels, including supermarkets, clinics, hospitals, pharmacies, and drugstores.

In this role, you will be responsible for managing and optimizing our information systems, ensuring seamless integration and functionality across the organization. Your expertise will be crucial in supporting our operational efficiency and enhancing our technological capabilities to better serve our customers.

At SASMAR, we value a collaborative and inclusive workplace where each team member's contributions are recognized and valued.

Responsibilities


Manage and maintain the organization's information systems, ensuring data integrity and security
Support and troubleshoot end-user issues related to software applications and hardware
Collaborate with various departments to identify technology needs and develop solutions that enhance operational performance
Implement system upgrades and enhancements, as well as manage system documentation
Provide training and support to staff on software applications and information system processes
Monitor system performance and conduct regular assessments to ensure compliance with industry standards
Assist in the development and implementation of IT policies and procedures
Stay up-to-date with emerging technologies and recommend strategies for continuous improvement


Requirements


Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field
2-4 years of experience in information systems management or a similar role
Strong knowledge of database management systems and software applications
Experience with system integration and data analysis tools
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to effectively collaborate with various teams
Ability to manage multiple priorities and work under pressure in a fast-paced environment
Proficiency in Google Office Suite and familiarity with Odoo project management software
Knowledge of compliance and regulatory standards relevant to the pharmaceutical industry is an advantage
Certifications in IT or related areas (such as ITIL, CompTIA, or Microsoft) are a plus
Legally able to work in Malta
This position has an immediate start. No work permit - applicants must have European citizenship or have long term residency


Benefits


Competitive salary
English language and multi-cultural work environment
Private Health Insurance (medical, dental, hospitalisation)
Career advancement opportunities in stable company
Modern offices, convenient to public transport.


This position is based at our new villa offices in Ta' Xbiex.

Title: Market Intelligence Specialist
Description:
La figura di Market Intelligence Specialist ha come scopo quello di creare e condividere una profonda conoscenza del mercato delle costruzioni al fine di supportare il processo decisionale strategico negli ambiti di marketing e di business con un approccio data-driven.

All’interno della funzione di Market Intelligence & CRM, questo ruolo fornisce insight approfonditi e raccomandazioni strategiche riguardo alle dinamiche e agli stakeholder del mercato in cui opera l’azienda, con un focus particolare su trend e dimensioni del mercato, strategie e prezzi dei concorrenti, nonché bisogni e comportamenti dei clienti.




Responsabilità:




Analizzare e monitorare trend di settore, individuando e condividendo potenziali opportunità di sviluppo o rischi di mercato
Strutturare ed eseguire attività di sizing del mercato delle costruzioni per specifici segmenti del settore, individuando ed utilizzando fonti di dati affidabili
Condurre studi di mercato per comprendere a fondo le dinamiche di specifici segmenti, esplorare nuove geografie, supportare lo sviluppo prodotto, identificare i bisogni dei clienti e testare l’accettazione da parte dei clienti di nuovi prodotti o servizi
Predisporre modelli di previsione per generare e aggiornare le proiezioni del settore delle costruzioni e dei suoi specifici segmenti in funzione dell’andamento economico, geopolitico e governativo
Raccogliere e analizzare i prezzi dei concorrenti per supportare la definizione delle politiche di prezzo e sconto dell’offerta aziendale




Qualifiche:




Laurea in Economia, Business Management, Ingegneria Gestionale o similari
Esperienza di 3-5 anni, maturata preferibilmente in contesti BtoB, l'aver maturato esperienza nel settore costruzioni/edilizia sarà considerato un plus
Competenze avanzate in analisi di mercato e dei trend di settore
Capacità di gestione di ricerche qualitative/quantitative e interpretazione di insight data-driven
Capacità di sviluppo e aggiornamento di modelli previsionali
Esperienza nell'analisi di benchmark, pricing e posizionamento di mercato
Capacità nella definizione di raccomandazioni strategiche basate sui dati e di presentare insight in modo chiaro ed efficace
Ottima capacità di lavoro in team, di negoziazione e di comunicazione
Forte orientamento ai risultati e capacità di problem solving
Capacità di gestire le priorità e rispettare le scadenze
Ottima conoscenza della lingua Inglese e disponibilità a traferte su territorio nazionale e internazionale
Ottima conoscenza del pacchetto Office, in particolare di Excell

La sede di lavoro è fissata presso l'Headquarter Kerakoll in via dell'Artigianato 9 a Sassuolo




Cosa ti offriamo

Un percorso di crescita professionale e formazione continua.
Un ambiente di lavoro stimolante, dinamico e flessibile orientato all’innovazione.
Retribuzione annua lorda (RAL): range compreso tra € 40.000 e € 50.000.
La Retribuzione Annua Lorda (RAL) sarà erogata in n. 13 mensilità.
CCNL applicabile: Industria Chimica.
Bonus: sistemi di incentivazione variabile pensati per riconoscere concretamente il contributo delle persone, collegati a obiettivi aziendali chiari, trasparenti e misurabili, che valorizzano i risultati e l’impegno di ciascuno.
Welfare aziendale: un insieme di iniziative e strumenti pensati per supportare il benessere complessivo delle persone e delle loro famiglie, offrendo soluzioni concrete in ambiti come salute, previdenza, istruzione e tempo libero.
Flessibilità al servizio delle persone: modalità di lavoro flessibili, come lo smart working per i ruoli elegibili, e altri strumenti che favoriscono un equilibrio sostenibile tra vita professionale e personale.




Kerakoll si impegna a offrire un pacchetto retributivo equo e competitivo, costruito con attenzione e trasparenza, in coerenza con i principi di equità interna e posizionamento di mercato. Al termine del processo di selezione, l’offerta sarà definita valorizzando l’esperienza, le competenze e le qualifiche della persona candidata, in relazione ai requisiti del ruolo e alle fasce retributive previste.

Ulteriori dettagli saranno condivisi nelle fasi successive, nel rispetto delle Policy Aziendali applicabili.




Cosa significa lavorare in Kerakoll

In Kerakoll ci impegniamo a creare un ambiente di lavoro basato su fiducia, inclusione e rispetto, ispirato ai principi di lealtà, trasparenza, sostenibilità ed efficienza. Valorizziamo il patrimonio di conoscenze, esperienze e competenze che ogni persona porta con sé, considerandolo un elemento essenziale di eccellenza e competitività. Promuoviamo politiche meritocratiche che riconoscono e sviluppano qualità e potenziale individuale, impegnandoci a garantire equità e inclusione in ogni fase del percorso professionale. I nostri processi di selezione si fondano esclusivamente su conoscenze e competenze, nel pieno rispetto delle pari opportunità quindi non accettiamo alcuna forma di discriminazione legata ad etnia, nazionalità, genere, età, disabilità, orientamenti sessuali, opinioni politiche, sindacali o convinzioni religiose e condizioni personali.

Infine, collaboriamo con partner che condividono i nostri valori, per attrarre i migliori talenti e costruire una comunità coesa, in cui ciascuno possa sentirsi valorizzato e libero di esprimere il proprio potenziale.




Unisciti a noi!

Title: Impiegato/a data entry – Settore Logistica e Distribuzione
Description:
Struttura operativa della Grande Distribuzione Organizzata seleziona seleziona una risorsa da inserire nel team amministrativo per attività di inserimento dati e gestione documentale.

La figura fornisce supporto operativo ai reparti interni, assicurando precisione e aggiornamento costante delle informazioni relative a prodotti, clienti e fornitori.

Attività principali

Inserimento e aggiornamento dati relativi a ordini, prodotti e clienti nei database aziendali

Verifica e correzione di informazioni contenute in fogli di calcolo e documenti operativi

Gestione dell’anagrafica di clienti e fornitori, con monitoraggio e aggiornamento periodico

Collaborazione con logistica e ufficio commerciale per organizzazione e archiviazione dei dati di produzione e distribuzione

Requisiti richiesti

Diploma di scuola secondaria superiore

Buona conoscenza di Excel e degli strumenti informatici di base

Precisione e capacità di lavorare in autonomia

Attitudine al rispetto delle scadenze e capacità di collaborazione in team

Offerta e benefit

Contratto a tempo determinato di 6 mesi

Orario full-time: dal lunedì al venerdì (8:30–12:30 / 15:30–19:30)

Graduale acquisizione delle competenze richieste attraverso affiancamento operativa in sede

Offerta e benefit:


Retribuzione 1690-2310 € lordi mensili

Title: Addetto/a allo Sportello - Servizi alle Imprese
HTML:
Società attiva nel settore dei servizi alle imprese, impegnata nella gestione e ottimizzazione delle attività interne, seleziona un/una Addetto/a allo Sportello.

La risorsa sarà inserita all’interno del team operativo e si occuperà di accogliere i clienti e gestire le richieste presso lo sportello, garantendo un servizio puntuale ed efficiente e supportando le attività interne dell’ufficio.

Mansioni principali

Accoglienza e assistenza clienti presso lo sportello;

Gestione delle richieste e dei contatti tramite telefono, email e strumenti digitali;

Supporto nelle pratiche amministrative e nella gestione dei documenti;

Inserimento e aggiornamento dei dati nei sistemi gestionali aziendali;

Collaborazione con i reparti interni per assicurare un servizio coordinato e puntuale.

Requisiti richiesti

Diploma di scuola superiore;

Buone capacità comunicative e relazionali;

Precisione, puntualità e attenzione ai dettagli;

Familiarità con strumenti digitali e applicativi informatici di base;

Predisposizione al lavoro in team e orientamento al cliente.

Offerta

Contratto a tempo determinato di 3 mesi;

Formazione retribuita sulle procedure e sui sistemi aziendali.

Title: Senior BI Data Analyst (Product)
HTML:
pawaTech is looking for a Senior BI Data Analyst (Product) to join our Business Intelligence team, with a strong focus on our Casino product. In this role, you will help turn data into meaningful insights that support product decisions. From understanding player behaviour and profitability to analysing feature performance and customer journeys, your work will help shape the future of our Casino product.


You will partner with stakeholders across the business, identify opportunities for improvement, build reports and dashboards, and ensure our teams have the data they need to make informed decisions. If you enjoy solving business problems through data, working closely with product teams, and influencing strategic decisions, we would love to hear from you.


Compensation range: €4000 - €4700 monthly gross.


About us:

pawaTech is a leading iGaming solutions provider, delivering sportsbook, casino, lottery, and player management software as a service. We power betPawa - Africa’s largest betting brand, operating across multiple markets.


Our platform is designed for scale, handling millions of active users across the web and mobile, processing thousands of transactions every second. With nearly a decade of industry expertise, we don’t just build software - we craft tailored solutions that drive innovation. Our expertise, commitment to quality, and client-focused approach make us a trusted partner in Africa and beyond.


What you’ll be doing:

  • Analysing player behaviour, game performance, profitability, and customer engagement across our Product (Casino) to identify trends and opportunities;
  • Building and maintaining dashboards, reports, and data visualisations that support product and business decision-making;
  • Performing ad hoc analysis to answer business questions and evaluating the performance of games, features, and promotions;
  • Developing and improving product metrics, KPIs, and analytical reporting to help drive the growth of our Casino product;
  • Working closely with different teams to understand reporting requirements and ensuring the availability of accurate and meaningful data;
  • Contributing to the development of analytical datasets, reporting structures, and ETL processes;
  • Validating data quality and helping ensure the accuracy and consistency of reporting;
  • Presenting analytical findings and recommendations to both technical and non-technical stakeholders;
  • Proactively suggesting new metrics and analytical approaches that support business growth.


What makes you a great candidate:

  • At least 5 years of experience in Business Intelligence, Product Analytics, Data Analytics, or a similar role;
  • Experience working with Casino or iGaming products in a start-up or scale-up environment;
  • Strong SQL skills with experience working with large and complex datasets;
  • Experience using Python for data analysis;
  • Experience with data visualisation tools such as Tableau, Redash, Power BI, or similar platforms;
  • Experience working with cloud-based analytical platforms such as BigQuery or Google Cloud Platform;
  • Strong analytical thinking with the ability to translate data into meaningful business insights and recommendations;
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills, with confidence discussing data with both technical and non-technical stakeholders;
  • Comfortable working with developers, data engineers, and business stakeholders to define reporting requirements and analytical solutions;
  • Ability to work independently, proactively suggest improvements, and thrive in a fast-paced, flat organisation.


Extra expertise we appreciate:

  • Hands-on experience with ClickHouse;
  • Experience working with Airflow or Google Cloud Run;
  • Experience building product and business dashboards from large datasets.


Why choose pawaTech?

  • We make an impact and contribute to shaping the iGaming culture;
  • We offer growth inside dedicated teams of seniors and career prospects;
  • We embrace a modern technological stack and innovative approaches;
  • We apply AI-driven tools to enhance productivity, streamline workflows, and keep our teams ahead in innovation;
  • We value balance - a hybrid environment, wellness-related perks and team events are waiting for you;
  • We play it fair with compensation that recognises and backs your achievements, team budgets, and milestone recognition bonuses.



This job is accepting ongoing applications and there is no application deadline.


At pawaTech, we are dedicated to building a welcoming and diverse workplace and invite applicants from all backgrounds, regardless of race, gender, age, or other protected characteristics. Our hiring process ensures fairness, objectivity, and respect for every applicant. Applicants are welcome to redact or omit information from their resumes that identifies their age, date of birth, or dates of attendance or graduation from educational institutions.


In compliance with EU GDPR standards and PDPA in Estonia, your personal data will be treated confidentially, used purely for recruitment, and accessible only to authorised hiring team members. Please visit our candidate privacy policy to learn more. For additional information regarding the processing of your data, please reach out via data.protection@pawatech.com

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Why Grasp

Grasp is an agentic AI platform for finance. The $1.4 trillion financial advisory industry relies heavily on costly and scarce cognitive labor. The industry is so inefficient that advisory firms can charge millions of dollars for teams of junior employees, whose work is 90% manual and repetitive. It is clear that AI will automate large parts of this work and provide a massive uplift in productivity.

Grasp already counts 250+ of the world's largest financial institutions among its customers, spanning investment banks, private equity firms, and management consultancies including all Big 4, to automate complex company and market research workflows. We're growing 4x per year and are backed by globally renowned investors such as Octopus Ventures and Yanno Capital.

As part of our core technical team, you will be working on the absolute frontier of AI, building an LLM-based product with very strong customer traction.

Role

At Grasp, we are at the forefront of developing AI-driven solutions that reshape professional services. Our AI relies on high-quality, well-structured data, making the role of a Data Engineer crucial to our success. We are seeking talented individuals to join our team in Stockholm, Sweden. The position is onsite, and we offer relocation assistance for candidates.

Your responsibilities will include:


Data Pipeline Engineering: Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines that ensure the seamless flow and availability of diverse legal datasets for our AI models. You will craft systems that are robust and efficient, facilitating easy access and optimal use for analysis and AI deployment.
Database Management and Performance Tuning: Take charge of our database environments to maximize efficiency in data storage and retrieval. Your focus will be on optimizing performance to support high-speed data operations while ensuring the utmost in data integrity and security.
Data Structuring and Optimization: Develop innovative strategies for data chunking and parsing to enhance our RAG pipelines.
Product Enhancement: Work closely with AI engineers to refine and advance our data infrastructure, aligning it with evolving AI capabilities and business needs.
System Evaluation and Iteration: Implement and manage systems to monitor and evaluate the health and performance of our data processes. Identify areas for improvement, and continuously iterate on solutions to enhance data handling and utilization, ensuring alignment with our overall AI strategy.


Skills and requirements

For this role you should:


Have a strong track-record of building AI/ML-based systems or products.
Have experience in building with LLMs - both using existing models and training/fine-tuning new models.
Have experience in building information retrieval or RAG pipelines.


Additionally, we think you will thrive in this role if you:


Are excited about building the future of AI systems in knowledge work.
Like to move fast, with a high sense of urgency in getting things done.
Are a self-starter and have a high degree of self-directedness.
Have excellent problem-solving and analytical skills.


What we offer


A unique opportunity to join a fast-growing startup in a once-in-a-lifetime moment in technology (AI).
A great opportunity to learn and develop both on a professional and personal level.
Lots of responsibility from day one.
A competitive compensation package, incl. stock options.

Title: Junior Data Engineer
HTML:
Ben jij gefascineerd door AI, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot en slimme automatiseringen? Vind je het leuk om data om te zetten in waardevolle inzichten én wil je AI-oplossingen ontwikkelen die collega's dagelijks slimmer laten werken? Dan is dit jouw kans.

Binnen Jarola Group groeit de behoefte aan betrouwbare data, goede dashboards en slimme digitale oplossingen. Daarom zoeken wij, ter uitbreiding van ons team Data & Analytics, een Junior Data Engineer die samen met ons de volgende stap wil zetten.

Wat ga je doen?

Als Junior Data Engineer maak je onderdeel uit van het team Data & Analytics. Je werkt nauw samen met onze BI Ontwikkelaar, Teamleider Data & Analytics en collega's uit de hele organisatie.

In jouw functie als Junior Data Engineer ondersteun je het team bij het ontwikkelen en verbeteren van Power BI rapportages, help je collega’s met datavraagstukken en werk je mee aan nieuwe AI-toepassingen. Je onderzoekt hoe AI, ChatGPT, Copilot, Claude en andere AI tools onze bedrijfsprocessen kunnen verbeteren, bouwt aan proof-of-concepts en vertaalt ideeën naar praktische oplossingen voor de toekomst.

Jouw groeipad.

Wij geloven dat je het meest leert door actief samen te werken met collega’s en stap voor stap meer verantwoordelijkheid te krijgen. Daarom hebben we een duidelijk ontwikkelpad opgesteld waarmee je doorgroeit naar een zelfstandige rol.

Een sterke basis in Power BI & Business Intelligence

In deze fase leer je onze organisatie, dataomgeving en rapportages goed kennen en werk je nauw samen met onze BI Ontwikkelaar. Je houdt je o.a. bezig met:

  • Leren werken met onze datasets, datamodellen en BI-omgeving;
  • Bouwen, aanpassen en verbeteren van Power BI-rapportages en dashboards;
  • Oppakken van gebruikersvragen rondom rapportages en data;
  • Uitvoeren van testwerkzaamheden en opstellen/verbeteren van documentatie.


AI, automatisering & Data Analytics

Wanneer je de basis beheerst, ga je in deze fase steeds intensiever samenwerken met het volledige team Data & Analytics. De nadruk verschuift van rapportages naar innovatie. In deze fase ga je:

  • AI use-cases onderzoeken en uitwerken;
  • Proof-of-concepts ontwikkelen met Artificial Intelligence;
  • Experimenteren met ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Claude en andere AI-tools;
  • Processen automatiseren met behulp van AI en moderne Microsoft-technologie;
  • Data-analyses uitvoeren voor verschillende afdelingen.


Zelfstandig Data & AI Developer

Na verloop van tijd groei je door naar een zelfstandige rol waarin je verantwoordelijk bent voor data- en AI-oplossingen die direct bijdragen aan de verdere digitalisering van Jarola Group. Je bent o.a. verantwoordelijk voor:

  • Signaleren van kansen om AI en data slimmer in te zetten;
  • Ontwikkelen van AI-oplossingen, dashboards en data-oplossingen;
  • Bouwen van procesautomatiseringen;
  • Samenwerken met Sales, Marketing, Finance, IT en Operations aan innovatieve projecten.


Dit ben jij!

Je bent nieuwsgierig naar nieuwe technologie en vindt het leuk om dingen uit te proberen. Misschien gebruik je ChatGPT al dagelijks of experimenteer je thuis met AI-tools. Ervaring met Power BI, SQL, Python, Microsoft Fabric, Azure of Databricks is mooi meegenomen, maar geen harde eis. We vinden je leervermogen, nieuwsgierigheid en manier van denken minstens zo belangrijk.

Je herkent jezelf in het volgende:

  • Je hebt hbo werk- en denkniveau, bijvoorbeeld richting ICT, Informatica, Business IT, Data Analytics, Technische Bedrijfskunde of vergelijkbaar;
  • Je hebt affiniteit met Business Intelligence, Power BI of data-analyse;
  • Je hebt interesse in Artificial Intelligence, Generative AI en AI-tools zoals ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Claude en vergelijkbare toepassingen;
  • Je vindt het leuk om nieuwe technologie te ontdekken en toe te passen;
  • Je werkt nauwkeurig, gestructureerd en oplossingsgericht;
  • Je kunt technische onderwerpen begrijpelijk uitleggen aan collega's;
  • Je beheerst de Nederlandse taal goed;
  • Je bent beschikbaar voor 40 uur per week.


Dit krijg je van ons

Jarola Group is een gezond, nuchter en groeiend familiebedrijf waar veel kansen liggen. Binnen Data & Analytics werk je aan oplossingen die direct impact hebben op de organisatie. Daarnaast krijg je:

  • Een salaris tussen de €3.242,- en - €4.548,- afhankelijk van kennis en ervaring;
  • 25 vakantiedagen en 8% vakantiegeld op jaar- en fulltime basis;
  • Een contract voor 12 maanden met uitzicht op vast;
  • Ruime trainings- en opleidingsmogelijkheden;
  • Veel ruimte om jezelf te ontwikkelen richting Power BI, data en AI;
  • De kans om mee te bouwen aan een groeiende Data & Analytics afdeling;
  • Leuke personeelskortingen op onze producten;
  • Een goede pensioenregeling en WGA-verzekering;
  • Flexibele werktijden en deels thuiswerken behoort tot de mogelijkheden;
  • Reiskostenvergoeding van €0,23 per kilometer vanaf 10 kilometer enkele reis woon-werkverkeer.


Maak kennis met Jarola Group

Jarola Group is een dynamisch kennis- en handelsplatform dat producenten, wederverkopers, verwerkers en eindgebruikers met elkaar verbindt. Wij distribueren hoogwaardige technische producten via onze verkoopkanalen Wildkamp en Jarola en ontwikkelen daarnaast eigen verkoopconcepten en productmerken zoals Rawinso, SummerRain en Limso.

Naast dat we sterk groeien en ontwikkelen, zijn we ook nog steeds een gezellig, no-nonsense familiebedrijf. Dat merk je aan de korte lijnen, de ruimte voor eigen initiatief en de manier waarop collega’s samenwerken.

Ik ben enthousiast. Hoe nu verder?

Laat je gegevens achter via de sollicitatieknop. Na beoordeling van je sollicitatie nemen we binnen 5 werkdagen contact met je op.

Heb je eerst nog vragen over de functie? Neem dan contact op met Louwrens Engbersen, Teamleider Data & Analytics via telefoonnummer (0)524 74 95 08

Title: Voditelj odjela u trgovini, Zagreb
Description:
Dodatne informacije



ŠTO ĆEŠ RADITI?

Kao voditelj odjela, bit ćeš lider koji podržava i ohrabruje cijeli svoj tim. Njegujući inkluzivnu kulturu, koja potiče timski rad i poduzetnički duh, stvorit ćeš okruženje u kojem svi mogu napredovati. Prateći naše vrijednosti, pridonosit ćeš osobnom uspjehu, ali i uspjehu kompanije.



Također ćeš:



Voditi svoj tim u pružanju i postizanju izvanrednog iskustva kupovine, osiguravajući izvrsne operativne i vizualne standarde, dok aktivno potičeš i prodaju naših artikala.
Preuzimati odgovornost za prednosti svoje trgovine i odjela, analizirati prodajne rezultate, identificirati komercijalne mogućnosti, postavljati ciljeve i izrađivati planove za optimizaciju rezultata, profita i razine zaliha.
Upravljati planiranjem tima i rasporeda, osiguravati besprijekorne rutine otvaranja i zatvaranja trgovine.
Zapošljavati, obučavati i razvijati svoj tim uz poticanje inkluzivne kulture te timskog rada koji potiču rast i inovaciju.
Biti u toku s modnim trendovima, preferencijama kupaca te konkurentnim brendovima, kako bi se održala konkurentska prednost.
Ostavljati pozitivan dojam, sebe i brenda, tijekom svake interakcije s kupcima.

S KIM ĆEŠ SURAĐIVATI?



Naše trgovine su srce naše kompanije, u kojima se kupci direktno susreću s našim brendom. Kao dio dinamičnog tima u trgovini, svaka uloga – od prodajnih savjetnika, voditelja odjela, voditelja trgovine i vizualnih dekoratera, do glavnog blagajnika – doprinosi stvaranju inspirativnog i gostoljubivog okruženja. Također, surađivat ćeš s timom sa šireg područja, kao i između trgovina, kako biste podijelili iskustva i zajedno postigli uspjeh. Zajedno sa svojim timom, imat ćeš ključnu ulogu u pružanju usluga kupcima kako bi se osjećali samouvjereno te izrazili svoju individualnost uz najnovije trendove i beskrajne stilske mogućnosti. Izgradnjom značajnih veza s kupcima u našim trgovinama, modu činimo dostupnom i održivom za sve.



TKO SI?



Tražimo ljude s/sa...​



Iskustvom u prodajnom menadžmentu i operativnosti.
Stručnošću u planiranju, analizi i praćenju prodaje, koristeći alate usmjerene na kupce te izvještavanje.

I ljude koji su...​



Snažne i samouvjerene vođe, koji nadahnjuju, podučavaju i razvijaju svoje timove, a istovremeno predstavljaju vrijednosti i kulturu naše kompanije.
Uživaju u timskom i dinamičnom okruženju, te čiji je način razmišljanja usmjeren na pronalazak rješenja.
Motivirani za stvaranje sjajnog iskustva kupovine, uz promicanje i poticanje prodaje.
Istinski zainteresirani za modne trendove, komercijalnost i uvide u stanje konkurenata, kako bi se potaknula prodaja.
Učinkoviti komunikatori, kreativni i znatiželjni.
Fleksibilni, analitični i orijentirani k postignućima, s umijećem statističkog upravljanja te radom s brojevima.

TKO SMO?

Čine nas tisuće strastvenih i talentiranih kolega, ujedinjenih zajedničkom kulturom te vrijednostima. Zajedno želimo iskoristiti svoju snagu, svoje postignuće i znanje kako bismo usmjerili modnu industriju prema inkluzivnijoj i održivijoj budućnosti.

U H&M Grupi, ponosni smo na to što smo energična i gostoljubiva kompanija. Našim zaposlenicima nudimo atraktivne pogodnosti s opsežnim mogućnostima razvoja diljem svijeta.

Naša jedinstvenost proizlazi iz kombinacije mnogih faktora– naše inkluzivne i suradničke kulture, naših snažnih vrijednosti i prilika za rast no ponajviše naših ljudi koji nas čine onim što jesmo.​



Ovo je radno mjesto s punim radnim vremenom, sa sjedištem u Zagrebu

Prijavi se slanjem životopisa što je prije moguće, a najkasnije do 06.08.2026. Radi politike o zaštiti podataka, prijave prihvaćamo samo putem stranice karijere.

Show more
HTML:
Dodatne informacije

ŠTO ĆEŠ RADITI?

Kao voditelj odjela, bit ćeš lider koji podržava i ohrabruje cijeli svoj tim. Njegujući inkluzivnu kulturu, koja potiče timski rad i poduzetnički duh, stvorit ćeš okruženje u kojem svi mogu napredovati. Prateći naše vrijednosti, pridonosit ćeš osobnom uspjehu, ali i uspjehu kompanije.

Također ćeš:

  • Voditi svoj tim u pružanju i postizanju izvanrednog iskustva kupovine, osiguravajući izvrsne operativne i vizualne standarde, dok aktivno potičeš i prodaju naših artikala.
  • Preuzimati odgovornost za prednosti svoje trgovine i odjela, analizirati prodajne rezultate, identificirati komercijalne mogućnosti, postavljati ciljeve i izrađivati planove za optimizaciju rezultata, profita i razine zaliha.
  • Upravljati planiranjem tima i rasporeda, osiguravati besprijekorne rutine otvaranja i zatvaranja trgovine.
  • Zapošljavati, obučavati i razvijati svoj tim uz poticanje inkluzivne kulture te timskog rada koji potiču rast i inovaciju.
  • Biti u toku s modnim trendovima, preferencijama kupaca te konkurentnim brendovima, kako bi se održala konkurentska prednost.
  • Ostavljati pozitivan dojam, sebe i brenda, tijekom svake interakcije s kupcima.

S KIM ĆEŠ SURAĐIVATI?

Naše trgovine su srce naše kompanije, u kojima se kupci direktno susreću s našim brendom. Kao dio dinamičnog tima u trgovini, svaka uloga – od prodajnih savjetnika, voditelja odjela, voditelja trgovine i vizualnih dekoratera, do glavnog blagajnika – doprinosi stvaranju inspirativnog i gostoljubivog okruženja. Također, surađivat ćeš s timom sa šireg područja, kao i između trgovina, kako biste podijelili iskustva i zajedno postigli uspjeh. Zajedno sa svojim timom, imat ćeš ključnu ulogu u pružanju usluga kupcima kako bi se osjećali samouvjereno te izrazili svoju individualnost uz najnovije trendove i beskrajne stilske mogućnosti. Izgradnjom značajnih veza s kupcima u našim trgovinama, modu činimo dostupnom i održivom za sve.

TKO SI?

Tražimo ljude s/sa...​

  • Iskustvom u prodajnom menadžmentu i operativnosti.
  • Stručnošću u planiranju, analizi i praćenju prodaje, koristeći alate usmjerene na kupce te izvještavanje.

I ljude koji su...​

  • Snažne i samouvjerene vođe, koji nadahnjuju, podučavaju i razvijaju svoje timove, a istovremeno predstavljaju vrijednosti i kulturu naše kompanije.
  • Uživaju u timskom i dinamičnom okruženju, te čiji je način razmišljanja usmjeren na pronalazak rješenja.
  • Motivirani za stvaranje sjajnog iskustva kupovine, uz promicanje i poticanje prodaje.
  • Istinski zainteresirani za modne trendove, komercijalnost i uvide u stanje konkurenata, kako bi se potaknula prodaja.
  • Učinkoviti komunikatori, kreativni i znatiželjni.
  • Fleksibilni, analitični i orijentirani k postignućima, s umijećem statističkog upravljanja te radom s brojevima.

TKO SMO?

H&M Group je globalna kompanija snažnih modnih brendova i pothvata. Cilj nam je dokazati kako ne postoji kompromis između iznimnog dizajna, pristupačnih cijena i održivih rješenja. Želimo modu svima učiniti dostupnom, a naši kupci su u središtu svake naše odluke koju donosimo.

Title: Ingénieur Data (H/F)
Description:
Prêt à transformer la vision en action ?



Magellan est un Groupe international de Conseil en management et de Technologies. Notre force réside dans notre capacité à activer à la fois expertise métier et maîtrise technologique pour accompagner les entreprises et les organisations publiques dans toutes leurs transformations.



VISION IN ACTION



Dans un environnement où les mutations s'accélèrent, les organisations doivent en permanence réinventer leurs modèles pour rester compétitives. Pour y répondre, nous accompagnons nos clients de la définition de leur stratégie à sa mise en œuvre opérationnelle, en combinant expertise métier, maîtrise technologique et capacité d'exécution.



Rejoindre Magellan, c'est intégrer un collectif de 6 700 talents engagés, unis par une même exigence : comprendre les enjeux dans toute leur complexité pour construire des solutions concrètes et activables au plus près des réalités du terrain.



Magellan est classé 3e Best Workplace France (Palmarès Great Place To Work 2024) et n°1 des entreprises où faire accélérer sa carrière (Classement Linkedin Top Companies 2025).





Description du poste



L'industrialisation des plateformes Data (pipelines, ETL, qualité, gouvernance) sur les environnements cloud GCP ou AWS constitue un chantier structurant pour les organisations data-driven. Ce poste s'adresse à un ingénieur Data confirmé, capable d'opérer en coordination avec les équipes Data Scientists et Analysts.



💡 VOS MISSIONS



En tant qu'Ingénieur Data (H/F), vous concevez et développez les pipelines de données au sein d'un environnement technique exigeant (SQL/NoSQL, GCP, BigQuery, PowerBI, Terraform, GitHub, Python).



Concevoir et développer des pipelines de données robustes : collecte, traitement, stockage
Optimiser les flux de données pour améliorer l'efficacité et la performance
Gérer les infrastructures de bases de données SQL et NoSQL en environnement Cloud GCP ou AWS selon les principes CI/CD
Intégrer les données en développant des processus ETL et en assurant l'interopérabilité entre différents systèmes et sources
Être garant de la qualité des données : implémenter des contrôles de qualité et de cohérence, des processus de nettoyage et de validation
Échanger avec l'ensemble de l'équipe Data (Scientist et Analyst) pour accompagner et répondre aux besoins métier


Qualifications



Vous possédez déjà une solide expérience de 4 ans en Data dans des environnements cloud GCP/AWS et DevOps. Orienté solution vous êtes également bon communiquant et pédagogue envers les équipes. Maîtrisez des compétences en programmation (Python, SQL, etc.), en gestion de bases de données et en ingénierie logicielle. La connaissance des architectures Big Data est essentielle. Vous êtes curieux et faites de la veille sur les méthodes de développement, technologies et architectures





Informations supplémentaires



En rejoignant Magellan, vous intégrerez des équipes pluridisciplinaires et contribuerez à des projets concrets, au cœur des grandes transformations : IA générative, plateformes intelligentes, impératifs sociaux et environnementaux, etc.



Ce que Magellan vous garantit : une expérience professionnelle humaine, apprenante et épanouissante.

Title: Addetto/a Sportello – Supporto Operativo GDO
HTML:
Struttura attiva nella Grande Distribuzione Organizzata ricerca una risorsa da inserire presso lo sportello operativo del punto vendita, con funzioni di gestione delle comunicazioni interne, organizzazione degli appuntamenti e supporto alle attività amministrative di sede.

Attività principali

gestione del centralino e smistamento delle chiamate verso i reparti del punto vendita;

registrazione, presa in carico e organizzazione degli appuntamenti interni;

supporto nelle attività di gestione documentale e aggiornamento dei dati su gestionale;

assistenza ai reparti operativi per la preparazione e l’archiviazione di documenti;

attività generali di segreteria a supporto delle funzioni di sede.

Requisiti richiesti

buone capacità comunicative e relazionali;

conoscenza degli strumenti informatici di base (Office, posta elettronica, browser);

organizzazione, precisione e gestione autonoma delle attività;

capacità di interazione con più reparti e predisposizione al lavoro in team;

attitudine al problem solving e affidabilità nel ruolo.

Offerta e condizioni

contratto a tempo determinato di 3 mesi, con possibilità di proroga;

orario full time, 40 ore settimanali distribuite dal lunedì al venerdì;

buoni pasto;

scontistiche dedicate per il personale.

Offerta e benefit:

  • Retribuzione 1650-2230 € lordi mensili

Title: IT Junior
Description:
La D’Agostino Costruzioni, azienda operante nel settore delle Grandi Opere Infrastrutturali di rilievo nazionale, con committenti pubblici e privati, ricerca un/una IT Junior da inserire nel proprio organico per nuove commesse in fase di avviamento.

Supporto ai sistemi di rete e infrastruttura IT
Collaborazione all’implementazione di nuovi sistemi e soluzioni tecnologiche

Diploma o laurea in ambito informatico o affine
Conoscenza di base dei sistemi operativi Windows
Conoscenze di networking (LAN/WAN)
Buone capacità di problem solving e predisposizione al lavoro in team

Costituiscono titolo preferenziale:

Conoscenza di ambienti server

Esperienze di stage o tirocini in ambito IT

Si offre:

Inserimento in un contesto strutturato e in forte crescita
Percorso di formazione e affiancamento
Contratto e inquadramento commisurati all’esperienza

Title: Consultor/a técnico en Área de Justicia (AAPP)
Description:
Quieres empezar un nuevo reto profesional? ¡Tenemos una nueva oportunidad para ti!

En ALTIA llevamos más de 30 años creando soluciones digitales preparadas para el futuro, capaces de generar valor real y provocar cambios significativos.

Somos un equipo de más de 4.000 profesionales, presentes en más de 30 localizaciones de 10 países, que desde 1994 suman su energía y aplican su visión innovadora de la tecnología a proyectos realmente relevantes para más de 1.000 organizaciones que son motor del cambio.

Nos mueve un propósito claro: crecer haciendo crecer, y hacerlo de forma sostenible y duradera. Creemos que solo somos importantes si juntos generamos un impacto positivo y evolucionamos en el proceso.

¿Qué buscamos?

Queremos que te unas a nuestro equipo como Consultor/a de Innovación Digital, dentro del Área de Justifica en el sector Público

Funciones orientadas a la transformación digital, modernización estratégica y gobernanza tecnológica

¿Qué te ofrecemos?

Posibilidad de teletrabajo, con reuniones muy puntuales en oficina

Puesto estable con perspectivas de crecimiento según tus intereses profesionales.

Plan de formación continua que te permitirá evolucionar en tu carrera profesional.

Formarás parte de un equipo consolidado y con experiencia.

Trabajarás en proyectos de alto impacto.

Retribución flexible y competitiva.

Si te apasiona la tecnología ¡este es tu lugar!

Requisitos mínimos

Titulación de Grado, Licenciado o Ingeniero Superior en las áreas de ingeniería, informática o ciencias.

Experiencia de mas de 10 años en Área Jurídica, dentro de la Innovación Digital, modernización e implantación de sistemas complejos, etc..

La presente oferta de empleo está redactada teniendo en cuenta la imparcialidad y no discriminación por razón de género, raza, ideología o cualquier otro motivo. Especialmente, se tiene en cuenta el respeto a la normativa vigente relativa a la igualdad de género entre mujeres y hombres (Real Decreto-ley 6/2019).

Title: Senior Data Engineer
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Senior Data Engineer based in Austria.

As a Senior Data Engineer, you will own the complete data lifecycle, from ingestion and infrastructure to modeling, analytics, and business insights. Working in a fully remote, high-growth environment, you will design scalable data platforms that support strategic decision-making across product, finance, and growth teams. This role offers the opportunity to build resilient, production-grade data systems while leveraging modern cloud technologies and AI-powered development tools. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional stakeholders, solve complex data challenges at scale, and play a critical role in shaping a data-driven organization. If you enjoy end-to-end ownership, continuous innovation, and building systems that can grow alongside the business, this is an excellent opportunity to make a lasting impact.

Accountabilities


Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines from source systems through data warehouses and analytics platforms.
Develop and optimize data ingestion workflows using cloud-native technologies, orchestration tools, and Python-based data loaders.
Create and maintain reliable data models that enable accurate reporting, business intelligence, and self-service analytics.
Deliver actionable analyses that explain business performance and support strategic decision-making.
Build data infrastructure with scalability, reliability, performance, and cost optimization as core design principles.
Manage infrastructure as code to ensure consistent, automated deployment and maintenance of the data platform.
Partner closely with product, finance, growth, and engineering teams to translate business questions into trusted data solutions.
Leverage AI-powered development tools to improve engineering productivity while maintaining full ownership of production quality.
Monitor, troubleshoot, and continuously improve data pipelines and platform performance.
Stay current with emerging technologies and best practices in data engineering, cloud infrastructure, and artificial intelligence.


Requirements


5+ years of experience designing, building, and operating production-grade data pipelines and modern data platforms.
Strong expertise with cloud data technologies such as Google Cloud Platform, BigQuery, dbt, Airflow, Dataflow, or equivalent solutions.
Experience with infrastructure as code using tools such as Pulumi or Terraform.
Advanced SQL skills and proficiency in Python for data engineering and automation.
Experience working with analytics platforms and transforming complex datasets into business insights.
Familiarity with AI-assisted development tools and an understanding of how to apply them responsibly within engineering workflows.
Strong analytical thinking with the ability to identify root causes behind business metrics and performance changes.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across technical and non-technical teams.
Comfortable working independently in a fast-paced, remote-first environment with a high level of ownership.
Passion for scalable data engineering, continuous learning, and building reliable systems that support business growth.


Benefits


Fully remote position with the flexibility to work from anywhere.
Co-working space membership or allowance to support your preferred work environment.
Equity participation through an employee stock ownership program (ESOP).
Technology allowance to build your ideal home office setup.
Comprehensive health insurance, including full employee coverage and substantial dependent coverage.
Flexible, asynchronous work schedule that promotes autonomy and work-life balance.
Annual professional development budget for courses, conferences, books, and learning resources.
Annual company off-site events that bring together global team members for collaboration and networking.
Opportunity to work with modern cloud technologies, large-scale data systems, and cutting-edge AI tools.
Collaborative, international environment focused on innovation, ownership, and continuous growth.


How Jobgether Works

Title: Ingest Specialist - English (EU)
Description:
About Simply.TV

At Simply.TV, we're redefining how the world experiences TV metadata. As a leading provider of advanced, AI-driven metadata solutions, we empower broadcasters, streaming platforms, and telecom operators to deliver smarter, more engaging content discovery experiences. From next-gen EPG (Electronic Program Guide) data to rich content enrichment, we enable our partners to stay ahead in a rapidly evolving media landscape.

Headquartered in Copenhagen, but supported by 400+ employees worldwide, our data fuels user interfaces that are used by millions of viewers every day — and we're just getting started. Whether you're passionate about media technology, data engineering, or product innovation, Simply.TV is the place to shape the future of TV — and have fun doing it.

Join us, and let's make content discovery simple, powerful, and inspiring!

The Role

Simply.TV is all about creating the world's best and richest TV, movie and series data. With thousands of new titles releasing every month across global platforms, we need passionate people to help us build, maintain and continuously improve our content database — ensuring world-class user experiences for TV operators globally.

As an Ingest Specialist, you will oversee the technical entry point for our global metadata. You will proactively identify and diagnose pipeline interruptions, implementing rapid fixes to resolve data gaps. This position requires a proactive thinker who can navigate technical requirements with external partners and align them with our internal workflows to ensure seamless content delivery.

Responsibilities:


Lead the technical setup of new content feeds from initial datapoint to the final activation of automated ingest
Oversee ingest workflows to ensure all incoming content feeds and data deliveries meet strict internal and external SLAs
Identify, investigate, and resolve data ingest failures to ensure consistent metadata delivery across all pipelines
Coordinate with downstream stakeholders and provide technical support for client-related inquiries
Use internal ticketing systems to document bugs, provider-specific requirements and internal workflows


Requirements

You'll thrive in this role if you:


Have 1-3 years of experience in data operations, technical support, or a similar role focused on digital content pipelines and data management
Fluency in English with the capacity to communicate complex technical issues to diverse stakeholders clearly and concisely
Intermediate to advanced skills in Excel or Google Sheets, specifically regarding VLOOKUPs, data cleaning, and logical functions
Proven competence in end-to-end project execution and independent management of multiple workstreams
Ability to work within Central European Time (CET/CEST) business hours


Preferred:


Technical background in software, coding or related fields such as data systems, CMS or technical operations. Prior exposure to media or OTT environments, ingest pipelines or metadata QC is highly desirable
Strong understanding of digital systems, including file formats (XML, JSON, CSV) and data transfer protocols
Proven ability to quickly master proprietary internal tools and a high comfort level navigating various data formats and delivery standards
Proficiency in the German language is a significant advantage for supporting diverse regional data sources and partners


Please note - we can only accept CVs in English!

Why join?


🚀 Work at the forefront of media tech - Help shape how millions discover TV & streaming content
🧠 Sharpen your research & data skills - Gain hands-on experience with metadata, databases & quality control
🌍 Be part of a global, supportive team - Spread across the world, but rooted in the same values. At Simply.TV, everyone belongs and contributes in their own way. 400+ people, continuously building a culture together
🎯 Make a real impact - Your work ensures accurate, high-quality data used by top broadcasters & platforms
📈 Grow and learn every day - Fast-paced environment with room to develop and expand your role


Practicalities


Reports to: Head of Technical Account Management
Employment Type: Freelancer
Location: Poland/Romania/Moldova
Type of Collaboration: Freelancer setup ( Fulltime-40hrs/week)


Benefits

Collaboration based on a freelancer(contractor) set-up is up to 1000 EUR per month on a full-time allocation basis (40 hours per week).

Title: Programa de Estágios Profissionais Setembro 2026
HTML:
Constrói uma carreira connosco

O Programa de Estágios Profissionais foi desenhado para aqueles que, como tu, querem desenvolver competências para o futuro, adquirindo experiência numa área funcional de uma das maiores empresas de retalho do mundo. Em todos os momentos, terás acompanhamento, feedback e tutoria para que evoluas de forma consistente e desenvolvas novas competências.


O que podes esperar do programa?

  • Participação ativa num projeto com impacto real numa das áreas funcionais do Grupo Jerónimo Martins, como Comercial, Marketing, Sustentabilidade, Comunicação, Recursos Humanos, Logística, IT, Analytics, Financeira, Gestão de Projetos, entre outras;
  • Um percurso que pode ir de 6 meses a 1 ano, com possibilidade de posterior integração no Grupo, com momentos de desenvolvimento e uma equipa sempre pronta a apoiar-te;
  • Trabalhar com pessoas inspiradoras e com muita experiência, disponíveis para te dar feedback;
  • Um tutor dedicado, que te guiará ao longo de todo o percurso, garantindo que tens uma experiência enriquecedora;
  • Oportunidades de desenvolvimento através de iniciativas e contacto direto com o nosso negócio.


És quem procuramos se…

  • Tens licenciatura concluída e/ou mestrado em áreas relevantes para o contexto organizacional, como Gestão, Finanças, Economia, Contabilidade, Engenharia, Comunicação, Marketing, Recursos Humanos, Logística, Tecnologia, Ciência de Dados ou outras áreas relevantes;
  • Tens domínio das ferramentas do MS Office, especialmente Excel;
  • Tens capacidade analítica, pensamento crítico e orientação para resultados;
  • Gostas de trabalhar em equipa, tens iniciativa e vontade de aprender;
  • Tens disponibilidade para trabalhar a full-time.


Gostas de ter um papel ativo no negócio e de aprender enquanto contribuis com impacto real? Temos para ti a oportunidade de assumires um papel essencial no Grupo Jerónimo Martins.

Title: Data Quality Analyst
Description:
We’re Watts. Together, we’re reimagining the future of water.

We feel proud every day about what we do. We're all part of the same crucial mission, no matter what function we support -- it's to provide safe, clean water for the world, and to protect our planet's most valuable resource.

What We Do

For 150 years, Watts has built best-in-class products that are trusted by customers in residential and commercial settings across the world. We are at the forefront of innovation, working with cutting-edge technology to provide smart and connected, sustainable water solutions for the future. Watts is a leading brand with a quality reputation — and we have a dynamic future ahead.

Scope of Position

The Data Quality Analyst is accountable for ensuring the accuracy, completeness, consistency, and reliability of our data assets. They play a key role in maintaining high data quality standards by identifying issues, implementing data validation processes, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to improve data governance and integrity. This role partners closely with cross-functional teams—including Finance, Operations, Engineering, and Product Management

This position reports to Master Data Manager, Fluid Solutions. This role may be remote or hybrid, based in North Andover, MA.

Primary Job Duties And Responsibilities


Perform data profiling, validation, and cleansing to ensure high-quality datasets
Identify, investigate, and resolve data discrepancies and anomalies
Develop and maintain data quality metrics, rules, and dashboards
Collaborate with functional business groups, business analysts, and other stakeholders to address data issues
Monitor data pipelines and systems for consistency and accuracy
Support root cause analysis of data defects and recommend corrective actions
Document data quality standards, processes, and best practices
Participate in data governance initiatives and ensure compliance with policies
Perform regular audits to verify data integrity across systems
Automate data quality checks where possible using tools and scripts


Required Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field
2+ years of experience in data quality, data analysis, or similar roles
Strong SAP skills and experience working with relational databases
Proficiency in Excel and/or data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau)
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical-thinking skills
Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills


General Applicable Company Competencies


Commitment to Watts’ values of integrity, accountability, continuous improvement and innovation, and transparency.
Punctuality and dependability.
Ability to be flexible and adapt to changing work priorities and stressful conditions.
Adherence to all personnel policies, procedures, and standards of process as implemented by Watts.
Maintain productive and collaborative relationships with other Watts employees.
Adherence to Watts’ seven cultural beliefs: Growth Mindset, Customer-Focused Innovation, Constant Communication, Clear Goals, Collaborate Globally, Be Inclusive, and Take Action.


Working Conditions

While performing the job duties, you will be working in an office environment. You will be required to work in the office at the North Andover location three days per week (Monday – Wednesday) and can work remotely two days per week (Thursday and Friday).

Physical Requirements: Specific physical abilities required for this position include, but are not limited to:


Ability to remain seated at a desk or workstation for extended periods.
Ability to perform repetitive tasks like typing on a keyboard or using a mouse for extended periods.
Ability to physically move around the office, organize or transport files, packages, or other office-related materials.
Ability to read documents, use a computer, and perform data entry tasks.
Ability to communicate clearly with management and coworkers, particularly in meetings or phone calls.
Ability to operate standard office equipment such as computers, printers, phones, and copiers.
Ability to occasionally lift and carry light objects, such as office supplies, documents, or small equipment.


Pay Range

The expected salary range for this position is $24.00 - $30.00/hr. Actual compensation will be dependent upon individual skills, experience, qualifications, and applicable law.

Nothing in this job description restricts Watts’ right to assign or reassign duties, responsibilities, and working hours/conditions to this position at any time. This position is “at will,” which means that either the employee or Watts may terminate the employment relationship at any time, with or without notice, and for any lawful reason.



Watts In It For You

Please note that the following benefits apply only to permanent roles and do not apply to internship roles.


Competitive compensation based on your skills, qualifications and experience
Comprehensive medical and dental coverage, retirement benefits
Family building benefits, including paid maternity/paternity leave
10 paid holidays and Paid Time Off
Continued professional development opportunities and educational reimbursement
Additional perks such as fitness reimbursements and employee discount programs
Learn more about our benefit offerings here: https://tapintowattsbenefits.com/


How We Work

At Watts, our culture is team-oriented and supportive. Employees here genuinely care about the quality of their work, and about each other. Our people are the heart of who we are and contribute to our longevity and continued success.

And this is a place where you can have a big career. No matter your role, there are opportunities for learning and development, and your daily contributions make a meaningful impact on the lives of people who use our products and on the future of water.

Watts is committed to equal employment opportunity. We follow a policy of administering all employment decisions and personnel actions without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, pregnancy, national origin, sexual orientation, age, physical or mental disability, genetic disposition or carrier status, marital status, military or veteran status, minorities, or any other category protected under applicable federal, state, or local law. Consistent with the obligations of state and federal law, Watts will make reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. Any employee who needs a reasonable accommodation should contact Human Resources.

  • Ability to be flexible and adapt to changing work priorities and stressful conditions.
  • Adherence to all personnel policies, procedures, and standards of process as implemented by Watts.
  • Maintain productive and collaborative relationships with other Watts employees.
  • Adherence to Watts’ seven cultural beliefs: Growth Mindset, Customer-Focused Innovation, Constant Communication, Clear Goals, Collaborate Globally, Be Inclusive, and Take Action.

Working Conditions

While performing the job duties, you will be working in an office environment. You will be required to work in the office at the North Andover location three days per week (Monday – Wednesday) and can work remotely two days per week (Thursday and Friday).

Physical Requirements: Specific physical abilities required for this position include, but are not limited to:

  • Ability to remain seated at a desk or workstation for extended periods.
  • Ability to perform repetitive tasks like typing on a keyboard or using a mouse for extended periods.
  • Ability to physically move around the office, organize or transport files, packages, or other office-related materials.
  • Ability to read documents, use a computer, and perform data entry tasks.
  • Ability to communicate clearly with management and coworkers, particularly in meetings or phone calls.
  • Ability to operate standard office equipment such as computers, printers, phones, and copiers.
  • Ability to occasionally lift and carry light objects, such as office supplies, documents, or small equipment.

Pay Range

The expected salary range for this position is $24.00 - $30.00/hr. Actual compensation will be dependent upon individual skills, experience, qualifications, and applicable law.

Nothing in this job description restricts Watts’ right to assign or reassign duties, responsibilities, and working hours/conditions to this position at any time. This position is “at will,” which means that either the employee or Watts may terminate the employment relationship at any time, with or without notice, and for any lawful reason.



Watts In It For You

Please note that the following benefits apply only to permanent roles and do not apply to internship roles.

  • Competitive compensation based on your skills, qualifications and experience
  • Comprehensive medical and dental coverage, retirement benefits
  • Family building benefits, including paid maternity/paternity leave
  • 10 paid holidays and Paid Time Off
  • Continued professional development opportunities and educational reimbursement
  • Additional perks such as fitness reimbursements and employee discount programs
  • Learn more about our benefit offerings here: https://tapintowattsbenefits.com/

How We Work

At Watts, our culture is team-oriented and supportive. Employees here genuinely care about the quality of their work, and about each other. Our people are the heart of who we are and contribute to our longevity and continued success.

And this is a place where you can have a big career. No matter your role, there are opportunities for learning and development, and your daily contributions make a meaningful impact on the lives of people who use our products and on the future of water.

Watts is committed to equal employment opportunity. We follow a policy of administering all employment decisions and personnel actions without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, pregnancy, national origin, sexual orientation, age, physical or mental disability, genetic disposition or carrier status, marital status, military or veteran status, minorities, or any other category protected under applicable federal, state, or local law. Consistent with the obligations of state and federal law, Watts will make reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. Any employee who needs a reasonable accommodation should contact Human Resources.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Crown Linen Service, Inc is looking to add an ON-SITE Data Analyst to the team in Brockton, MA!

Join Crown Linen Service Inc as a Full-Time Data Analyst and immerse yourself in an environment where innovation meets excellence. Located in Brockton, MA, this onsite role offers the opportunity to tackle real-world problems and drive strategic initiatives. With a competitive pay range of $80,000 - $90,000, you will be rewarded for your analytical skills and contributions to our forward-thinking team. Experience the thrill of collaborating with passionate professionals dedicated to high performance and integrity in everything they do.

THIS IS NOT A REMOTE POSITION AND REQUIRES DAILY ON-SITE REPORTING TO OUR BROCKTON, MA LOCATION.

You will be provided with great benefits such as Medical, Dental, Vision, 401(k), Life Insurance, Health Savings Account, Flexible Spending Account, Competitive Salary, and Paid Time Off. Don’t miss the chance to shape the future of our services while advancing your career in a supportive and dynamic workplace.

Make a difference as a Data Analyst

We are seeking a Data Analyst to join our data team. We are a small team at a well-established company, giving us the opportunity to make a real and immediate impact. This role focuses on developing reports and automated dashboards to support multiple departments.

What We Offer


A nimble work environment that provides the freedom to explore our data landscape and discover new strategies
An executive team that values the insights that data can provide
A direct impact on the business via reporting, as well as data-driven strategies
A company culture that supports its workforce and is focused on operating an ethical and sustainable business.


What You Bring


Three to five (3-5) years of working experience with progressively more responsibility and advanced work product.
A curious mind - you will often be tasked with vague targets, and it is up to you how to get there.
A collaborative spirit - we touch every aspect of the business and need to be able to work seamlessly across all business functions.
A heightened business acumen - we are not just data folks. We regularly interact with all levels of the organization and must be able to relate our findings to executable business strategies.
A technically savvy approach.
Be an Excel wizard - much like an instrument, you should be able to play Excel like a fiddle
A comfortable working knowledge of how to query data sets across multiple sources with significant exposure to at least one SQL language, including Joins, CTEs, Aggregations and/or Windows Functions (our bread and butter)
The ability to transform vague business requests into concrete data driven solutions
Work experience with at least one BI tool such as PowerBI, Tableau, etc.
The ability to design, implement and maintain an ever-changing ETL landscape

As a new Data Analyst at Crown Linen Service Inc, you can expect a dynamic work environment where each day brings new challenges. Your schedule will be Monday to Friday, with standard business hours dedicated to analyzing data, identifying trends, and providing actionable insights to enhance our services. You will collaborate closely with various departments, facilitating cross-functional meetings to ensure alignment on projects and initiatives. Expect to create and maintain detailed reports, supporting strategic decision-making processes. Your role will involve problem-solving and innovation, requiring you to think critically and propose solutions that drive efficiency.

Additionally, you will participate in ongoing training and development opportunities, allowing you to continuously enhance your skills and knowledge in a high-performance culture.

Make your move

If you're ready to take your Data analyst skills to the next level and join a team that values hard work and your business analyst expertise, apply today!

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment.

Salary: $80000.00 - $90000.00 per year
Show more

Title: Systems and Analytics Specialist
Description:
Summary

Reporting to the Team Lead, Pharmacy Business Operations and Clinical Intelligence is intricately involved in the day to day technology integrated within all areas of pharmacy services across Brown University Health (i.e., clinical, operational, safety, quality, financial, regulatory, accreditation, supply chain, retail, academia). Develops custom reports and data visualizations. Maintains a data warehouse along with associated Extract Transfer and Load (ETL) processes. Monitors and improves existing business practices through use of technology and data.

Brown University Health employees are expected to successfully role model the organization's values of Compassion, Accountability, Respect, and Excellence as these values guide our everyday actions with patients, customers and one another.

In Addition To Our Values, All Employees Are Expected To Demonstrate The Core Success Factors Which Tell Us How We Work Together And How We Get Things Done. The Core Success Factors Include

Instill Trust and Value Differences

Patient and Community Focus and Collaborate

Responsibilities


Utilize technical, analytical, and programming skills to support all areas of pharmacy services (i.e., clinical, operational, safety, quality, financial, regulatory, accreditation, supply chain, retail, academia).
Maintain the pharmacy’s data warehouse.
Design, build and troubleshoot data Extract, Transfer Load (ETL) processes.
Design, build and troubleshoot highly functional data visualizations utilizing Microsoft PowerBI or similar tools.
Collaborate with pharmacy clinical and operational staff to understand health system needs and devise possible solutions.
Keep up to date with the latest technology trends.
Collaborate in the design on business processes to allow for appropriate and efficient data collection.
Work jointly with stakeholders through the healthcare organization.
Take part in and complete technological project work.
Maintain and troubleshoot internal webpages both interactive and static
Maintain and troubleshoot Microsoft Excel, Microsoft Access and Filemaker solutions.
Design and/or implement solutions to security vulnerabilities and concert with Brown University Health information systems security.
Serve as an expert in the functionality and troubleshooting of pharmacy technology systems.
Responsible for weekly rotating on call coverage.


Minimum Qualifications


Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Information Science, or related field.
3 years of experience with SQL, data analytics, and programming, or equivalent.
Strong background in writing SQL. Able to understand various data structures and common methods in data transformation.
Proven analytic and process redesign skills.
Highly proficient in Microsoft Office tools such as Work, Excel and Access.


WORK ENVIRONMENT AND PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS: Work is performed in a typical office environment, requiring extended periods of sitting, standing and walking. Requires the visual and manual dexterity to operate a computer.

INDEPENDENT ACTION: Functions independently within broad scope of department policies and procedures.

Refers specific complex problems to Team Lead and when clarification of departmental policies and procedures may be required.

SUPERVISORY RESPONSIBILITIES: None

Pay Range

$78,416.00-$129,355.20

EEO Statement

Brown University Health is committed to providing equal employment opportunities and maintaining a work environment free from all forms of unlawful discrimination and harassment.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking to hire a data analyst to join our data team. You will take responsibility for managing our master data set, developing reports, and troubleshooting data issues. To do well in this role you need a very fine eye for detail, experience as a data analyst, and a deep understanding of the popular data analysis tools and databases. Data Analyst Responsibilities: Managing master data, including creation, updates, and deletion. Managing users and user roles. Provide quality assurance of imported data, working with quality assurance analysts if necessary. Commissioning and decommissioning of data sets. Processing confidential data and information according to guidelines. Helping develop reports and analyses. Managing and designing the reporting environment, including data sources, security, and metadata. Supporting the data warehouse in identifying and revising reporting requirements. Supporting initiatives for data integrity and normalization. Assessing tests and implementing new or upgraded software and assisting with strategic decisions on new systems. Generating reports from single or multiple systems. Troubleshooting the reporting database environment and reports. Evaluating changes and updates to source production systems. Training end-users on new reports and dashboards. Providing technical expertise in data storage structures, data mining, and data cleansing. Data Analyst Requirements: Bachelor s degree from an accredited university or college in computer science. Work experience as a data analyst or in a related field. Ability to work with stakeholders to assess potential risks. Ability to analyze existing tools and databases and provide software solution recommendations. Ability to translate business requirements into nontechnical, lay terms. High-level experience in methodologies and processes for managing large-scale databases. Demonstrated experience in handling large data sets and relational databases. Understanding of addressing and metadata standards. High-level written and verbal communication skills.
