Title: DATA COORDINATOR
Description:
Description

Job Overview

The Entwistle Company is seeking a detail-oriented and highly organized Data Coordinator to build a long-term career in our collaborative and innovative environment.

In this role, you’ll help deliver mission-critical components that support U.S. and Allied defense, with your skills, creativity, and impact directly contributing to national and global security. We value talent, encourage innovation, and promote professional growth.

Ready to make a real difference? Join us.

Our Mission Statement


To be an essential provider of mission-critical defense components & systems, spanning air, land, sea and space, recognized for our specialized capabilities, quality and dependability.
To design, manufacture & build-to-print innovative and cost-effective products.
To help our DoD, Prime and Foreign Military customers and US warfighter succeed in their missions.


At The Entwistle Company, our values—Trust, Respect, Accountability, Collaboration, and Commitment—guide everything we do, from daily decisions to long-term strategy.

Key Responsibilities


Enter, update, and maintain procurement database, supplier recordkeeping per FAR and DFARS regulatory environment, verify pricing and part numbers in ERP/MRP systems.
Monitor and reconcile data inconsistencies in procurement documentation, contracts, and supplier communications.
Generate reports related to supplier performance, order status, lead times, and delivery metrics for internal use. Advanced reporting and KPI monitoring at the direction of Management.
Manage and maintain Safety Data Sheets.
Collaborate with Buyers, IS managers, and Supply Chain/Procurement management.
Assist with audit preparation and document preparation for regulatory or customer reviews.
Provide administrative support to the Purchasing team, including database, IS management, and document filing.


Qualifications


Associate’s degree in Supply Chain, Data Analytics, Computer Science or related field; Bachelor’s preferred.
2–4 years of experience in procurement or supply chain support, ideally in a defense manufacturing environment.
5+ years of demonstrated DBM/Advanced Excel including Pivot Tables and custom formula creation
Proficient in ERP Systems (Global Shop Solutions preferred) with strong data entry, reporting, and analysis skills.
Knowledge of manufacturing procurement processes, vendor management best practices, MRP, and defense industry compliance (FAR, DFARS).
Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple priorities while maintaining data accuracy and confidentiality.
Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively across departments.


Related Military Job Codes (HR USE)

We value military experience and recognize transferable skills. Candidates with the following military job codes or similar may have relevant experience for this position:


Army MOS: 51C – Acquisition, Logistics, and Technology NCO
Navy NEC: LS – Logistics Specialist
Air Force AFSC: 3A1X1 - Administration
Marine Corps MOS: 3043 – Supply Administration and Operations Specialist


What We Offer


401k – 4% match on 5% deferrals
PTO – Up to 5 weeks
Employer Paid Life Insurance
Employer Paid Short-term and Long-term Disability
Employer provided Paid Family/Medical Leave
Generous Health Insurance Coverage
Dental Insurance AND Vision Insurance
Safety Shoes & Glasses reimbursements
Employee Appreciation Events


Requirements

Competitive pay based on experience.

Title: Senior Data Analyst - Security Focus
Description:
Summary

MathWorks has a hybrid work model that enables staff members to split their time between office and home. The hybrid model provides the advantage of having both in-person time with colleagues and flexible at-home life optimizations. Learn More: https://www.mathworks.com/company/jobs/resources/applying-and-interviewing.html#onboarding.

The Data Strategy and Management Team within our IT department is seeking a highly skilled Senior Data Analyst with a strong focus on data architecture and security. This cross-functional role requires a proactive individual who can collaborate effectively with business partners and systems teams to ensure the integrity, quality, and security of our enterprise data.

MathWorks nurtures growth, appreciates inclusivity, encourages initiative, values teamwork, shares success, and rewards excellence.

Responsibilities


Support design for target state data architecture to manage data effectively across the enterprise with an initial focus on customer data.
Assist in the development and implementation of a data architecture governance model to ensure data is managed appropriately across the enterprise.
Assist in the analysis and development of a consistent data retention policy to reduce data exposure risk.
Evaluate Tools and Processes: Assess and recommend security and governance tools to achieve target state for customer data management.
Collaborate with Business, Information Security, and IT Stakeholders: Work with business stakeholders, product owner(s), the Information Security team, Legal, and agile development teams to understand existing data architectures, target state requirements, and impacts of proposed data architecture changes.


Minimum Qualifications


Visa sponsorship will not be provided for this position.
Candidates for this position must be authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis for any employer without restriction.
A bachelor's degree and 6 years of professional work experience (or a master's degree and 3 years of professional work experience, or equivalent experience) is required.


Additional Qualifications


Effective communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills. Communicates and presents complex ideas effectively to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Proficiency with data models, data analysis and data architecture principles.
Experience with data governance frameworks
Initiative-taking and curious nature, an independent self-starter.
Adept at cross-functional impact analysis, proactively collaborating across team boundaries to arrive at comprehensive and cohesive solutions.
Demonstrated ability to identify and analyze downstream impact of changes in complex systems.
Proven ability to elicit and document business requirements within an Agile framework, translating them into practical and clear user stories.
Proficiency in SQL skills


Pluses:


Experience with data governance frameworks, RBAC, and IAM solutions.
Familiar with system and data security concepts, certifications, data standards, and best practices in data systems context, CMMC, and data retention policies.
Experience with custom-built web applications, AWS, as well as platforms such as Salesforce and/or Snowflake.
Proficiency with tools such as Microsoft Power BI/ Tableau for data analysis and reporting.

  • Effective communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills. Communicates and presents complex ideas effectively to both technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Proficiency with data models, data analysis and data architecture principles.
  • Experience with data governance frameworks
  • Initiative-taking and curious nature, an independent self-starter.
  • Adept at cross-functional impact analysis, proactively collaborating across team boundaries to arrive at comprehensive and cohesive solutions.
  • Demonstrated ability to identify and analyze downstream impact of changes in complex systems.
  • Proven ability to elicit and document business requirements within an Agile framework, translating them into practical and clear user stories.
  • Proficiency in SQL skills

Pluses:

  • Experience with data governance frameworks, RBAC, and IAM solutions.
  • Familiar with system and data security concepts, certifications, data standards, and best practices in data systems context, CMMC, and data retention policies.
  • Experience with custom-built web applications, AWS, as well as platforms such as Salesforce and/or Snowflake.
  • Proficiency with tools such as Microsoft Power BI/ Tableau for data analysis and reporting.

Title: Senior Data Systems Analyst
Description:
ZoomInfo is where careers accelerate. We move fast, think boldly, and empower you to do the best work of your life. You’ll be surrounded by teammates who care deeply, challenge each other, and celebrate wins. With tools that amplify your impact and a culture that backs your ambition, you won’t just contribute. You’ll make things happen–fast.

ZoomInfo is seeking a Senior Data Analyst to become the expert on our business entity data pipeline—the system that ingests, processes, and profiles millions of company records that power our customers' go-to-market strategies.

In this role, you'll build deep expertise in how our business entity data flows from acquisition through profiling and output. You'll understand how data is transformed and processed through the pipeline, bring informed opinions to technical conversations, and help shape the evolution of our data quality capabilities. As you build mastery of our systems, you'll increasingly lead strategic data improvement initiatives that require both systems thinking and creative problem-solving.

This isn't about building dashboards or SQL reports. This is about understanding how data behaves as it moves through systems, solving complex data quality challenges, and ensuring our pipeline continuously evolves to meet customer needs and maintain competitive advantage.

Who You Are

Systems Thinker with Technical Depth: You understand how data systems work, not just what they produce. You've worked with data pipelines or ETL workflows and can review code (Python, Java, SQL) to understand data transformations and trace how data flows through systems.

B2B Data & GTM Domain Expert: You understand firmographic data and how sales and marketing teams use company attributes (employee counts, locations, hierarchies, technology usage, industry classifications) for targeting and segmentation. You've worked with B2B sales data or revenue operations and can evaluate data quality through both a technical and business lens.

Opinionated Technical Contributor: You have informed opinions on how things should work. You assess technical tradeoffs, evaluate solution feasibility, and contribute meaningfully to conversations with engineers based on your understanding.

Growth-Oriented Problem Solver: You're excited to build deep expertise in a complex domain and take on strategic projects. You've tackled ambiguous problems that required figuring things out as you went.

Analytical and Hands-On: You're equally comfortable writing code to analyze data patterns and manually investigating edge cases. You dig into details when needed and know when to zoom out to see the bigger picture.

What You'll Do

Build Data Quality Expertise


Master how our business entity data behaves as it flows through our pipeline: what transforms are applied at each stage, where quality issues emerge, and how changes impact downstream data
Review production code to understand how data is processed and assess how proposed changes would impact data quality
Create documentation and system maps that capture how data is processed, dependencies, and quality considerations


Drive Pipeline Improvements


Partner with Engineering and Product on pipeline enhancements, bringing data quality insights, business impact assessments, and informed opinions on data requirements
Validate improvements through rigorous testing and hands-on verification of data quality
Translate data quality investigations into system-level improvement opportunities


Solve Complex Data Challenges


Lead data improvement initiatives that require systems thinking and creative problem-solving (improving location verification, integrating new data sources, or solving novel extraction challenges)
Tackle ambiguous problems through understanding processing logic, manual investigation, and cross-functional coordination
Build repeatable approaches to testing, validation, and root cause analysis


Required

What You'll Bring


Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or related quantitative field
5+ years of experience in data analytics or related technical roles with focus on data quality and pipeline systems
Experience working with data pipelines, ETL systems, or data processing workflows where you understand how data moves through systems and what can go wrong
Strong skills investigating data quality issues through profiling data, identifying anomalies, tracing root causes, and validating fixes
Ability to understand data transformations by reviewing SQL, Python, Java, or similar code
Strong programming skills in Python and SQL for data analysis, profiling, and manipulation
Experience solving ambiguous, multi-faceted data problems that required figuring out the approach, not just executing well-defined analyses
Demonstrated ability to work effectively with Engineering and/or Product teams, translating between technical implementation and business needs
Self-directed with strong ownership mentality; you drive your work forward and know when to seek input


Strongly Preferred


Ability to work hybrid from our Vancouver, WA or Waltham, MA office locations
Experience working with firmographic/company data in B2B context
Experience with data profiling, entity resolution, record linkage, or data matching systems
Background in web data acquisition, data extraction, or data collection systems
Knowledge of web data structures (HTML/DOM) and data extraction methodologies
Experience designing validation strategies or testing frameworks for data quality
Experience creating technical documentation or knowledge-sharing resources
Track record of leading or co-leading cross-functional data projects


This Role Is Not a Fit If

You're looking to focus primarily on building dashboards, creating reports, or analyzing business metrics. While you'll do analysis in this role, the core mission is understanding and improving how business entity data is processed and profiled through our pipeline systems.

This Role Is a Strong Fit If

You want to become the expert on how business entity data flows through systems and drive strategic initiatives. You're excited to develop deep technical expertise in data quality, contribute informed opinions to data requirements, and progressively take on more ownership as you master the domain. You enjoy solving problems where the answer isn't obvious and seeing your work directly improve product capabilities and customer success.

Actual compensation offered will be based on factors such as the candidate’s work location, qualifications, skills, experience and/or training. Your recruiter can share more information about the specific salary range for your desired work location during the hiring process. We want our employees and their families to thrive.

In addition to comprehensive benefits we offer holistic mind, body and lifestyle programs designed for overall well-being. Learn more about ZoomInfo benefits here.

Below is the US base salary for this position. Additional compensation such as Bonus, Commission, Equity and other benefits may also apply.

$102,200—$160,600 USD

About Us

ZoomInfo (NASDAQ: GTM) is the Go-To-Market Intelligence Platform that empowers businesses to grow faster with AI-ready insights, trusted data, and advanced automation. Its solutions provide more than 35,000 companies worldwide with a complete view of their customers, making every seller their best seller.

Title: Junior Analyst - Commodities
Description:
Reily Foods Company has a passion for flavor and has been providing iconic foods and beverages that create meals and memories for over 120 years! Our products consist of New England Coffee, French Market Coffee, Luzianne Iced Tea, Blue Plate Mayonnaise, Swans Down Cake Flour, Tiger Sauce, and Carroll Shelby Chili (to name a few) and can be found in foodservice and retail locations around the country.

Job summary: The position acts in a support role to the Senior Commodities Director for a broad range of tasks required to manage the commodities supply chain. Tasks may include data analysis such as for raw material costs using data in Excel, and creating reports from existing files and creating new files as needed. Data analysis of commodity prices and purchase position. Assist in the preparation of forecasts and commodity coverage for Reily and private label customers. Other general administrative tasks as needed to assist the Senior Director.

Key Responsibilities


Data management for costs, sales, client volumes, budget
Internal communication to receive and supply information to internal colleagues and managers
Partnership management with external customers regarding administrative items and issues, such as volumes, pricing, etc.
Physical commodity activity including evaluation of coffee and tea products
Internal team communication and coordination with respect to department responsibilities and activities


We offer a comprehensive and thoughtfully designed benefits package that supports the well-being, financial security, and work-life balance of our employees.


10 Paid Company Holidays
Generous Paid Vacation & Sick Time
Comprehensive, Affordable Medical, Dental & Vision Coverage for you and your family
Health Savings Account (HSA) with company contributions
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) & Dependent Care FSA (DCFSA)
401(k) Retirement Plan with company match and discretionary profit sharing
Employee Assistance Program (EAP) and financial wellness resources
Company‑Paid Disability
Company‑Paid Life Insurance equal to 3× annual compensation
And more benefits designed to support your health, wealth, and well‑being


Together, these benefits reflect our commitment to caring for our employees both inside and outside the workplace.

Qualifications


Education: College degree or related experience is important to demonstrate general business skills to be successful in the position.
Experience: The candidate should have a minimum of two to five years’ business experience involving accounting, analytical, and administrative experience. This is preferably within a manufacturing environment.
Skills: Strong Excel and office programs experience is required. Good interpersonal or sales experience is important. Problem-solving skills required as well as the ability to work both independently and within a team.
Knowledge: Business experience, especially in commodities or food manufacturing is a plus.
Travel: Limited travel required.


Reily Foods Company is an equal opportunity employer, Minorities/Females/Disability/Protected Veteran/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity.

Title: Business Analyst II (Demand & Fulfillment)
Description:
At WHOOP, we're on a mission to unlock human performance and healthspan. We empower members to perform at their highest potential by providing deeper insights into their bodies and daily lives. Our team thrives on innovation, data-driven strategies, and a shared passion to redefine member engagement and retention.

We are seeking an Analyst to partner closely with our Operations team on understanding and optimizing daily decisions around demand and fulfillment. This role will partner closely with Demand Planning, Logistics & Fulfillment, Supply Chain and Finance to provide the analytical backbone for S&OP processes, demand planning and inventory decisions, and logistics optimization.

Responsibilities


Serve as the primary analytics partner for the monthly S&OP cycle, including demand velocity analysis, placed vs. fulfilled quantity comparisons, and demand acceleration/deceleration signals.
Analyze logistics data to identify cost drivers and service trade-offs. Use structured problem-solving and quantitative analysis to recommend process improvements that enhance efficiency and performance.
Investigate shipping delays, customs issues, and inventory discrepancies to identify root causes and deliver actionable recommendations
Build, maintain, and ensure accurate, reliable data and trusted metric definitions within our semantic layer and scalable solutions that enable stakeholders to confidently self-serve insights.
Manage multiple priorities with limited oversight, proactively communicating risks and timelines. Represent Analytics on cross-functional logistics initiatives and measure both qualitative and quantitative impact.


Qualifications


2+ years of experience in an analytical role with demonstrated ownership of metrics or data-driven initiatives
Demonstrated ability to distill complex datasets into clear and actionable insights and communicate them effectively to stakeholders.
Strong analytical skills, including proficiency in SQL and familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Sigma, Hex, or similar). Familiarity with modern data warehouses and analytics workflows (e.g., Snowflake, dbt) is a plus.
High intellectual curiosity and a self-starting mindset; comfortable working in a fast-paced, evolving environment.


This role is based in the WHOOP office located in Boston, MA. The successful candidate must be prepared to relocate if necessary to work out of the Boston, MA office.

Interested in the role, but don’t meet every qualification? We encourage you to still apply! At WHOOP, we believe there is much more to a candidate than what is written on paper, and we value character as much as experience. As we continue to build a diverse and inclusive environment, we encourage anyone who is interested in this role to apply.

WHOOP is an Equal Opportunity Employer and participates in E-verify to determine employment eligibility

The WHOOP compensation philosophy is designed to attract, motivate, and retain exceptional talent by offering competitive base salaries, meaningful equity, and consistent pay practices that reflect our mission and core values.

At WHOOP, we view total compensation as the combination of base salary, equity, and benefits, with equity serving as a key differentiator that aligns our employees with the long-term success of the company and allows every member of our corporate team to own part of WHOOP and share in the company’s long-term growth and success.

The U.S. base salary range for this full-time position is $100,000 - $125,000. Salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within each range, individual pay is based on factors such as job-related skills, experience, performance, and relevant education or training.

In addition to the base salary, the successful candidate will also receive benefits and a generous equity package.

These ranges may be modified in the future to reflect evolving market conditions and organizational needs. While most offers will typically fall toward the starting point of the range, total compensation will depend on the candidate’s specific qualifications, expertise, and alignment with the role’s requirements.

Title: Managed Care Data Analyst II
Description:
Site: Mass General Brigham Incorporated

Mass General Brigham relies on a wide range of professionals, including doctors, nurses, business people, tech experts, researchers, and systems analysts to advance our mission. As a not-for-profit, we support patient care, research, teaching, and community service, striving to provide exceptional care. We believe that high-performing teams drive groundbreaking medical discoveries and invite all applicants to join us and experience what it means to be part of Mass General Brigham.

Job Summary

Responsible for extracting knowledge and insights from data in order to investigate business/operational problems through a range of data preparation, modeling, analysis, and/or visualization techniques.

Essential Functions


Collects, analyzes, and reports data to support decisions on day-to-day operations, strategic planning, and/or specific business performance issues.
Collates, models, interprets, and analyzes data.
Explains variances and trends in data.
Identifies and documents enhancements to modeling techniques.
Completes thorough quality assurance procedures, ensuring accuracy, reliability, trustworthiness, and validity of work.
Performs data validation of source-to-target data for data visuals and dashboards.


Qualifications

Education


Bachelor's Degree in analysis or related field required. Equivalent experience may be accepted in lieu of degree


Experience


2+ years of experience required working with data, preferably healthcare data on the provider/payer side
Experience in financial analysis preferred


Knowledge, Skills And Abilities


Healthcare knowledge, particularly as it pertains to electronic medical record data, is preferred but not required.
Working knowledge of relational databases, SQL, data visualization, and Business Intelligence tools such as Tableau.
Knowledge and application of statistical analyses, including variance analysis and statistical significance, are preferred.
Project management skills and/or experience are a plus.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite, including Word, Excel and PowerPoint.


Working Schedule


Full time remote hours, optional onsite at Assembly Row in Somerville, MA
Remote hours require a quiet, secure, HIPAA-compliant working station


Additional Job Details (if Applicable)

Remote Type

Remote

Work Location

399 Revolution Drive

Scheduled Weekly Hours

40

Employee Type

Regular

Work Shift

Day (United States of America)

Pay Range

$63,648.00 - $90,750.40/Annual

Grade

6

At Mass General Brigham, we believe in recognizing and rewarding the unique value each team member brings to our organization. Our approach to determining base pay is comprehensive, and any offer extended will take into account your skills, relevant experience if applicable, education, certifications and other essential factors. The base pay information provided offers an estimate based on the minimum job qualifications; however, it does not encompass all elements contributing to your total compensation package. In addition to competitive base pay, we offer comprehensive benefits, career advancement opportunities, differentials, premiums and bonuses as applicable and recognition programs designed to celebrate your contributions and support your professional growth. We invite you to apply, and our Talent Acquisition team will provide an overview of your potential compensation and benefits package.

EEO Statement

0100 Mass General Brigham Incorporated is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, national origin, sex, age, gender identity, disability, sexual orientation, military service, genetic information, and/or other status protected under law. We will ensure that all individuals with a disability are provided a reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. To ensure reasonable accommodation for individuals protected by Section 503 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, the Vietnam Veteran’s Readjustment Act of 1974, and Title I of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990, applicants who require accommodation in the job application process may contact Human Resources at (857)-282-7642.

Mass General Brigham Competency Framework

At Mass General Brigham, our competency framework defines what effective leadership “looks like” by specifying which behaviors are most critical for successful performance at each job level. The framework is comprised of ten competencies (half People-Focused, half Performance-Focused) and are defined by observable and measurable skills and behaviors that contribute to workplace effectiveness and career success. These competencies are used to evaluate performance, make hiring decisions, identify development needs, mobilize employees across our system, and establish a strong talent pipeline.

  • 2+ years of experience required working with data, preferably healthcare data on the provider/payer side
  • Experience in financial analysis preferred

Knowledge, Skills And Abilities

  • Healthcare knowledge, particularly as it pertains to electronic medical record data, is preferred but not required.
  • Working knowledge of relational databases, SQL, data visualization, and Business Intelligence tools such as Tableau.
  • Knowledge and application of statistical analyses, including variance analysis and statistical significance, are preferred.
  • Project management skills and/or experience are a plus.
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite, including Word, Excel and PowerPoint.

Working Schedule

  • Full time remote hours, optional onsite at Assembly Row in Somerville, MA
  • Remote hours require a quiet, secure, HIPAA-compliant working station

Remote

40

Regular

Day (United States of America)

Title: Associate Manager, Data & Analytics
Description:
Permanent Full Time

The Associate Manager, Data & Analytics plays a key role in enabling data-driven decision making across the Health Products team, which is responsible for the direction, delivery and performance of Canada Life’s health and well-being products.

This role partners with leadership and cross-functional teams to define reporting needs, deliver standardized dashboards and insights, and translate complex data into clear, executive-ready outputs. The Associate Manager supports strategic and operational priorities through advanced analytics, while continuously improving data processes, governance, and reporting capabilities to drive consistency, efficiency, and business impact.

What will you do?

Stakeholder management / communication


Partner with Health Products leadership and cross-functional teams to define reporting needs, KPIs, and success metrics.
Engage business stakeholders early to understand requirements, clarify definitions, and align on outputs.
Translate complex data into clear, concise insights and executive-ready materials.


Reporting & data management


Design and maintain standardized reports, dashboards, and data models to support ongoing business needs.
Extract, clean, and integrate data from multiple sources (e.g., claims, policy, digital) to ensure consistency and usability.
Establish and document data definitions, logic, and governance to enable a single source of truth.


Analytics & insight generation


Analyze data to identify trends, drivers, and anomalies across health products and member behaviors.
Provide actionable insights and recommendations that inform strategic and operational decisions.
Support ad hoc analysis to answer key business questions and quantify opportunities.


Delivery & continuous improvement


Own and prioritize a portfolio of reporting and analytics deliverables, ensuring quality and timeliness.
Automate and enhance reporting processes to improve efficiency, scalability, and reliability.
Continuously identify opportunities to evolve analytics capabilities, tools, and outputs to better support the business.


What do you need to succeed?


5+ years of relevant experience and a university or college degree in a related field of study, or an equivalent combination of education and experience
Experience working with raw data and manipulating large datasets
Proficiency in Excel, Power BI, data modelling, and Databricks, with experience creating interactive dashboards
Ability to work with diverse data inputs and build data marts aligned with business requirements
Proven ability to learn quickly, work independently, and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
Ability to work with little to no documentation or instructions
Strong attention to detail, with the ability to produce high-quality outputs under tight timelines
Driven to continuously improve processes, performance, and efficiency
Knowledge of the organization, distribution channels, products, systems, and data is an asset
Ability to write complex SQL queries to extract and integrate data from multiple sources
Proficiency in scripting languages such as Python or R
Hands-on experience with Azure compute services and a good understanding of Azure Data Factory pipelines
Preferred: Microsoft Certified – Azure Data Analyst Associate (Power BI) certification


The base salary for this position is between $90,800 - $115,800 annually. This represents base salary only and does not represent other variable compensation components of our total compensation ( i.e. annual bonus, commission etc). If you are selected to move forward in our recruitment process, your recruiter will be able to discuss additional details of our total rewards program with you.

Career opportunities will be open a minimum of 5 business days from the date of posting, closing dates will vary depending on the search activity. All applications received will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

Grow with Canada Life

We’re united by a shared purpose: to improve the financial, physical and mental well-being of Canadians. Our company is trusted by 1 in 3 Canadians and contributes to the strength of communities across the country.

We’re looking for people who live our values everyday: we step up, we do the right thing, and we deliver – for our customers, communities and each other. Are you someone who always strives to do the right thing, who steps up for themselves and others, and who delivers with impact? Then we want to hear from you!

What we offer:

We’re committed to supporting our employees through every stage of their career. Here’s what you can expect as a full-time or part-time permanent team member:


Career Development: Opportunities for career advancement, access to industry-leading learning programs and up to $2,000 annually towards education reimbursement.

Title: Pricing Analyst
Description:
The Pricing Analyst is instrumental in providing key analytical, maintenance, and costing support to the organization. You will help drive efficiencies within the organization and drive profitable growth into the future.

As the Pricing Analyst you are a real Data Intelligence Analyst. This role is instrumental in providing key analytical, maintenance and costing support to the organization. Specifically, you will become the catalyst which will drive efficiencies within the organization. Your detail-oriented nature will promote and sustain the profitable growth of the company.

Responsibilities


Review market trends for pricing opportunities to optimize revenues
Monitor cost and raw material fluctuations
Interpret data into compelling insights and present to a variety of levels of leadership
Analyze price relationships between products
Manage new product listings in the system with appropriate cost and other key data elements
Assist in development of new key insights by constructing reports which can be utilized throughout the organization
Maintain data integrity within the system by conducting vendor reviews to ensure accurate information is present
Manage vendor price adjustments by uploading data within the system while maintaining accuracy
Maintain accurate vendor contracts and audit monthly denials


Requirements


Bachelor's degree with a specialization in Business or Mathematics
2-3 years previous experience in similar role
Microsoft Office expertise, especially in Excel (v-lookup, index match, Pivot table)
Excellent communicator – speaks and writes clearly and to the point
Analytical and organized with relentless attention to detail
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Team player who displays personal leadership
Basic knowledge of financial statements and trial balance information
Proven track record of process optimization an asset
Ability to work in a fast-paced national distribution environment


Bunzl Canada Inc. (bunzlcanada.ca) provides the food and retail packaging, cleaning and hygiene products and equipment, safety and industrial supplies which keep over 58,000 Canadian businesses running optimally, every day. We bring our customers the advantage of global sourcing, product innovation and national scale combined with responsive local service and deep category expertise. Bunzl Canada is a certified Great Place to Work®. We are committed to creating an inclusive, equitable and positive working environment for all employees so that they experience a strong sense of belonging, while developing individually and professionally. Bunzl Canada is an operating company of Bunzl plc (BNZL.L), a FTSE100 company listed on the London Stock Exchange. With 27,000 employees in over 30 countries, our global presence spans the Americas, Europe, Asia Pacific, and the UK and Ireland. At Bunzl, you’ll find Unlimited Potential… Your Career - - Our Future!

Bunzl Canada has a tradition of commitment to equal employment opportunity. It is our established policy to attract and retain the best qualified people without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex/gender (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, age, disability or veteran status as provided by law.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
About Us

At Glen Dimplex Americas, people drive our success. We design innovative comfort solutions by focusing on customers, caring for people, valuing innovation, and keeping things simple. Our portfolio—Dimplex, Cadet, and Convectair—delivers trusted electric heating, fireplaces, and controls across North America. As part of Glen Dimplex Group, we’re a global leader in electrical heating and comfort technology.

Learn more at: www.glendimplexamericas.com | www.dimplex.com | www.cadetheat.com | www.convectair.ca

Compensation

Base salary: $70,000 – $90,000

Reason for Opening

Existing Vacancy – Yes

Job Summary

The Business Intelligence Analyst reports to the Financial Planning and Analysis Manager and supports data analysis and reporting initiatives by aggregating data from multiple sources and developing insights to support business decision-making. This role focuses on building and maintaining reports, dashboards, and data solutions to enhance visibility into operational performance, sales, profitability, and customer behaviour.

Duties & Responsibilities


Support reporting and analytics initiatives in collaboration with FP&A and cross-functional teams
Assist in identifying and documenting existing reporting processes and contribute to standardization and simplification efforts
Develop an understanding of the business to support the definition and tracking of key performance indicators (KPIs)
Build, maintain, and enhance reports and dashboards using tools such as Power BI, IBM Cognos Analytics, and SAP
Support the automation of recurring reporting processes where possible
Assist in the development and maintenance of data marts and ETL processes
Monitor data quality and escalate issues to ensure accuracy and consistency
Maintain databases and reporting environments
Gather and translate business requirements into reporting and analytical solutions
Collaborate with stakeholders to deliver insights that support business decisions
Provide user support and training on reporting tools as needed
Respond to ad hoc reporting and analysis requests
Perform other related duties as assigned


Qualifications & Educational Requirements


Post-secondary degree in Computer Science, Analytics, or a related field
3–5 years of experience in a Business Intelligence, Data Analyst, or similar role
Experience in CPG or manufacturing environments considered an asset
Experience with Power BI, including DAX
Experience developing reports and dashboards
Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools


Technical Expertise


Working knowledge of:
Relational database design and data warehouse concepts
SQL, including writing and modifying queries
SSIS and SSAS fundamentals
Exposure to data modeling and ETL processes
Understanding of database structures and performance considerations

Business & Analytical Skills


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Effective communication skills with the ability to work with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Experience gathering and documenting business requirements


Preferred


Experience with SAP
Experience with Dynamics 365 CRM
Experience with Microsoft Azure
Understanding of financial principles and metrics
Experience working with financial data, forecasting tools, or reporting


Total Rewards

Core Total Rewards


Fully funded Group Benefits (medical, dental, vision, life insurance, dependent life, AD&D, short term disability, & long-term disability)
Group RRSP with 100% Employer Match up to 4%
Freedom to Choose, Consult +, and Telus Pharmacy Group
Employee Assistance Program
Workplace Wellness Program
Support of professional memberships/certifications


Additional Rewards


Educational Reimbursement Program
Employee Charitable Donation Program (monetary and product donations)
Employee Service & Recognition Awards
Referral Bonus Program
Summer Fridays
Employee Engagement Committee initiatives
Smart casual dress atmosphere
A unique opportunity to be a key player in a growing global organization
An innovative fast-paced culture focused on people


Our Values

We Think Customer – we aim to put our customers at the center of everything we do, taking time to understand their needs; creating solutions that meet and exceed their expectations, enhancing everyday moments

We Care About People – we recognize the power of a community with a common purpose; empathizing with others’ point of view and acting with integrity to create a safe and healthy environment

We Value Innovation – we are building a better future day by day; developing innovations that will create quality, consumer-centric, sustainable solutions

We Keep it Simple – we make the complex simple by driving out inefficiencies; which enables us to be agile, responsive and to get the job done

Equal Opportunity

Glen Dimplex Americas is committed to fair and accessible employment practices, including accommodations throughout the recruitment process. If you require accommodation, please let us know and we will work with you to meet your needs.

Please note, Glen Dimplex Americas may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools as part of our interview and recruitment process to assist in screening and evaluating applicants. All information will be handled in accordance with our privacy policies, and any AI-supported assessments are designed to ensure a fair and consistent evaluation for all candidates.

We thank all applicants for their interest in Glen Dimplex Americas; however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Retirement Analyst
Description:
We are searching for a Retirement Analyst to join our National Indigenous Solutions Practice in Winnipeg, Burlington, or Burnaby.

The Retirement Analyst plays a central role in advancing the Group Retirement practice within National Indigenous Solutions (NIS) Practice. This role blends data management, pricing support, market analysis, and insight generation to support both client retention and growth.

Working closely with the National Director, Group Retirement Strategy, the Retirement Analyst will act as a key contributor to how we collect, analyze, and apply data across a national block of business. The role is equal parts analytical and strategic. They will automate processes by leveraging AI, and deliver differentiated insights to Indigenous organizations and communities.

This is an excellent opportunity for a high-potential analyst to develop deep expertise in group retirement while helping shape a modern, data-driven practice.

We strongly encourage First Nations and Indigenous applicants to self-declare in your cover letter.

The Retirement Analyst Will

Data Management & Integrity


Maintain and validate client and member data across group retirement programs, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and completeness
Identify and resolve data gaps, inconsistencies, and anomalies across multiple data sources
Build and maintain structured datasets to support reporting, pricing, and insight generation
Establish disciplined processes to improve data quality and accessibility across the practice


Reporting & Benchmarking


Develop and maintain reporting frameworks and benchmarking models across the NIS block of business
Produce recurring and ad-hoc reports for client reviews, internal leadership, and new business opportunities
Leverage AI tools to automate reporting processes and enhance data visualization
Translate complex datasets into clear, client-ready insights that demonstrate value and market positioning


Pricing & New Business Support


Coordinate the preparation and management of group retirement quote requests with insurance carriers
Analyze pricing proposals, prepare comparisons, and support recommendation development
Build impactful, client-facing proposal materials for both existing and prospective clients
Support RFP responses and finalist presentations in partnership with business development and client management teams


Insights & Strategy Enablement


Analyze plan performance, member behaviour, and utilization trends to identify opportunities for improvement
Develop data-driven insights that inform plan design, investment menus, and member engagement strategies
Research industry trends and competitive positioning to strengthen NIS market differentiation
Utilize AI tools to accelerate analysis and surface insights across large and complex datasets


Operational Support & Collaboration


Partner closely with the National Director, Group Retirement Strategy, and broader NIS leadership team
Prepare materials for client meetings, plan reviews, and internal strategic discussions
Support governance processes and ensure readiness of documentation and reporting requirements
Contribute to a collaborative, high-performing team environment aligned to NIS priorities


To be successful as a Retirement Analyst with the National Indigenous Solutions Practice, you will need:


Minimum 2 years of experience in data analysis, financial services, group retirement, or a related field
Foundational understanding of group retirement programs, pricing, and reporting (or strong ability to learn quickly)
Advanced Excel skills and experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI)
Demonstrated hands-on experience using AI tools (e.g., Copilot, automation tools) to enhance analysis, improve workflows, and generate insights
Strong ability to work with large, multi-source datasets and translate findings into clear business narratives
Experience supporting pricing, proposals, or client-facing deliverables is an asset
High attention to detail, organization, and ability to manage multiple priorities
Strong communication and presentation skills, including building client-ready materials
Curiosity, adaptability, and a mindset of continuous improvement through technology


What’s In It For You


An opportunity to make a real impact in Indigenous communities across Canada
Involvement in meaningful work supporting Truth and Reconciliation efforts
Ongoing learning and collaboration with a supportive and experienced team
Competitive compensation
Comprehensive employee benefits and retirement programs
A fun, flexible, and productive work environment


Our Commitment

National Indigenous Solutions is committed to building a team that reflects the communities we serve. We welcome and encourage applications from First Nations, Métis, and Inuit candidates. Applicants are invited to self-declare Indigenous identity in their application.

The National Indigenous Solutions Practice, a division of People Corporation, is a trusted Group Insurance & Retirement practice proudly serving Indigenous employers across Canada. The National Indigenous Solutions Practice is comprised of three sister companies – Eagle Bay Financial (Burnaby, BC), Life Benefit Solutions (Winnipeg, MB) and WP Pension and Benefits (Burlington, ON) who all specialize in designing benefit and group retirement programs that thoughtfully integrate Non-Insured Health Benefits (NIHB), supporting equity between Status and non-Status employees. Their mission is to deliver customized, community-conscious solutions that meet the unique needs of Indigenous and non-Indigenous employees alike.

We are committed to fostering a respectful, inclusive, and accessible environment where all employees and clients feel supported and valued. We are proud to build a workforce that reflects the diversity of the communities we serve and to create an environment where everyone has the opportunity to thrive.

HTML:
We are searching for a Retirement Analyst to join our National Indigenous Solutions Practice in Winnipeg, Burlington, or Burnaby.

The Retirement Analyst plays a central role in advancing the Group Retirement practice within National Indigenous Solutions (NIS) Practice. This role blends data management, pricing support, market analysis, and insight generation to support both client retention and growth.

Working closely with the National Director, Group Retirement Strategy, the Retirement Analyst will act as a key contributor to how we collect, analyze, and apply data across a national block of business. The role is equal parts analytical and strategic. They will automate processes by leveraging AI, and deliver differentiated insights to Indigenous organizations and communities.

This is an excellent opportunity for a high-potential analyst to develop deep expertise in group retirement while helping shape a modern, data-driven practice.

We strongly encourage First Nations and Indigenous applicants to self-declare in your cover letter.

The Retirement Analyst Will

Data Management & Integrity

  • Maintain and validate client and member data across group retirement programs, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and completeness
  • Identify and resolve data gaps, inconsistencies, and anomalies across multiple data sources
  • Build and maintain structured datasets to support reporting, pricing, and insight generation
  • Establish disciplined processes to improve data quality and accessibility across the practice

Reporting & Benchmarking

  • Develop and maintain reporting frameworks and benchmarking models across the NIS block of business
  • Produce recurring and ad-hoc reports for client reviews, internal leadership, and new business opportunities
  • Leverage AI tools to automate reporting processes and enhance data visualization
  • Translate complex datasets into clear, client-ready insights that demonstrate value and market positioning

Pricing & New Business Support

  • Coordinate the preparation and management of group retirement quote requests with insurance carriers
  • Analyze pricing proposals, prepare comparisons, and support recommendation development
  • Build impactful, client-facing proposal materials for both existing and prospective clients
  • Support RFP responses and finalist presentations in partnership with business development and client management teams

Insights & Strategy Enablement

  • Analyze plan performance, member behaviour, and utilization trends to identify opportunities for improvement
  • Develop data-driven insights that inform plan design, investment menus, and member engagement strategies
  • Research industry trends and competitive positioning to strengthen NIS market differentiation
  • Utilize AI tools to accelerate analysis and surface insights across large and complex datasets

Operational Support & Collaboration

  • Partner closely with the National Director, Group Retirement Strategy, and broader NIS leadership team
  • Prepare materials for client meetings, plan reviews, and internal strategic discussions
  • Support governance processes and ensure readiness of documentation and reporting requirements
  • Contribute to a collaborative, high-performing team environment aligned to NIS priorities

To be successful as a Retirement Analyst with the National Indigenous Solutions Practice, you will need:

  • Minimum 2 years of experience in data analysis, financial services, group retirement, or a related field
  • Foundational understanding of group retirement programs, pricing, and reporting (or strong ability to learn quickly)
  • Advanced Excel skills and experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI)
  • Demonstrated hands-on experience using AI tools (e.g., Copilot, automation tools) to enhance analysis, improve workflows, and generate insights
  • Strong ability to work with large, multi-source datasets and translate findings into clear business narratives
  • Experience supporting pricing, proposals, or client-facing deliverables is an asset
  • High attention to detail, organization, and ability to manage multiple priorities
  • Strong communication and presentation skills, including building client-ready materials
  • Curiosity, adaptability, and a mindset of continuous improvement through technology

What’s In It For You

  • An opportunity to make a real impact in Indigenous communities across Canada
  • Involvement in meaningful work supporting Truth and Reconciliation efforts
  • Ongoing learning and collaboration with a supportive and experienced team
  • Competitive compensation
  • Comprehensive employee benefits and retirement programs
  • A fun, flexible, and productive work environment

Our Commitment

National Indigenous Solutions is committed to building a team that reflects the communities we serve. We welcome and encourage applications from First Nations, Métis, and Inuit candidates. Applicants are invited to self-declare Indigenous identity in their application.

The National Indigenous Solutions Practice, a division of People Corporation, is a trusted Group Insurance & Retirement practice proudly serving Indigenous employers across Canada. The National Indigenous Solutions Practice is comprised of three sister companies – Eagle Bay Financial (Burnaby, BC), Life Benefit Solutions (Winnipeg, MB) and WP Pension and Benefits (Burlington, ON) who all specialize in designing benefit and group retirement programs that thoughtfully integrate Non-Insured Health Benefits (NIHB), supporting equity between Status and non-Status employees. Their mission is to deliver customized, community-conscious solutions that meet the unique needs of Indigenous and non-Indigenous employees alike.

We are committed to fostering a respectful, inclusive, and accessible environment where all employees and clients feel supported and valued. We are proud to build a workforce that reflects the diversity of the communities we serve and to create an environment where everyone has the opportunity to thrive.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Artificial Intelligence. Actual Impact.

At Docebo, we’re using AI to change how people learn at work—and we mean actually change it. We’re an AI-powered learning platform that helps organizations create, deliver, and manage training all in one place. But our real mission goes deeper: we help teams move faster, work smarter, and focus on the work that truly matters. Our platform is built with intelligent, time-saving tools that personalize learning, eliminate busywork, and turn training from a checkbox into a superpower. The result? Better experiences for learners and real results for businesses.

We’re shaping the future of learning with a team that isn’t afraid to challenge the status quo. If you're excited by the idea of using AI to make work-life better for real people–you’ll feel right at home here. And it’s not just what we build, it’s how we show up. At Docebo, our values aren’t just posters on the wall—they guide how we work every day. We call it the Docebo Heart: trust by default, assume positive intent, and create space for different perspectives to thrive.

So… what are you waiting for? Join 900+ Docebians around the world and help us reinvent the way people learn, because learning never stops.

The Adventure Ahead 🚀

As a Data Analyst within the Product Engineering family, you will hold the keys to transforming raw information into the strategic compass that guides our entire organization. Reporting to the Manager of Data Analytics, your mission is to leverage company-wide data to generate actionable insights and build beautiful visualizations that enhance performance across the business. You will act as a critical collaborator with the Data team, serving as a vital contributor to our core data-driven decision-making processes while ensuring the ultimate accuracy and reliability of our data. This is your opportunity to turn complex datasets into a superpower that drives impactful, real-world business outcomes.

The Day-to-Day 📈


Decode the Data: Manipulate and analyze various complex datasets to uncover actionable insights and support key business decisions across the company.
Architect the Visuals: Design and deploy interactive dashboards using advanced data visualization tools to clearly visualize data and track performance metrics.
Bridge the Teams: Partner closely with the Data team and cross-functional teams across the organization to deeply understand business requirements and deliver tailored analytical solutions.
Predict the Future: Conduct advanced analytics, including predictive modeling, correlation analysis, and statistical evaluations to identify upcoming trends and strategic opportunities.
Guard the Governance: Implement rigorous data validation and governance processes to maintain the absolute highest standards of data accuracy and reliability.
Streamline and Automate: Reverse engineer data requirements to help Data Engineers streamline and automate data collection, processing, and reporting to improve efficiency and scalability.
Champion Clarity: Translate complex data into clear insights and actionable recommendations to improve business strategies, delivering findings through effective verbal and written communication for both technical and non-technical audiences.
Master the Databases: Query and manage databases with precision, utilizing tools and languages like SQL and Snowflake to extract and manipulate data for analysis.
Optimize the Engine: Continuously refine and improve reporting systems and workflows to align seamlessly with evolving business needs


Your Superpowers ⚡


The Data Track Record: 3+ years of proven experience in business intelligence, data analysis, or reporting, showing a passion for solving business problems through data.
The Academic Foundation: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Finance, Economics, Analytics, Business, Math, Statistics, or a related field.
The Tech Toolkit: Strong SQL and Python skills, with a proficiency in data visualization tools and their advanced functionalities (experience with Snowflake is a big plus!).
The Math Magician: Deep expertise in statistical analysis, data pivots, predictive modeling, and designing scalable data solutions.
The Detail Detective: High attention to detail, a detail-oriented mindset, and an unyielding commitment to maintaining data accuracy and quality.
The Proactive Collaborator: A self-motivated, proactive individual with a collaborative mindset who thrives in a fast-paced environment and is driven by process improvement and innovation.
The Master Communicator: Strong communication skills with a proven ability to deliver analytical insights effectively to diverse, cross-functional audiences.


Our Hybrid Work Philosophy 🤝

Great work can happen anywhere but coming together helps us go further. Our team spends three days a week in the office (Tuesday-Thursday) to collaborate, solve problems, and learn from each other. With flexibility the rest of the week, it’s a balance designed to help everyone do their best work and keep growing.

Our Total Rewards Philosophy 🎉

Our Total Rewards Philosophy centers around three core areas to reward and care for our People:


Rewarding Impact: We lead with competitive pay to reward the impact, skills and traits that fuel our success.
Fostering Holistic Wellbeing: We care deeply about and invest in the whole person with programs that support our people’s physical, mental, and financial well-being.
Empowering Our Talent Culture: We build a culture of trust and empowerment by designing our rewards and benefits with transparency, equity, and flexibility, enabling our people to do their best work and stay for the long haul.


Our Promise to You 😍


Financial Wellness: Own a piece of Docebo through our Employee Share Purchase Plan (ESPP) at a 15% discount, plus a competitive compensation package.
Your Well-Being, Covered: You’ll get access to health benefits, so you can get the care you need when you need it.
Rest, Relax, Repeat: Rest and recharge with paid vacation days, two company-wide Docebo Days, floating holidays for cultural celebrations, and your birthday off!
Family First: We provide coverage offering you time with your little one(s) so you can soak up all those precious moments. Fun fact: we had 30 Docebian babies join the family in 2025!
Connections That Count: Connect with global communities through our Employee Resource Groups (including PRIDE, DWA, BIDOC, and Green Ambassadors) and company-wide events that keep the fun rolling all year long.


About Docebo 💙

At Docebo, we create seamless, AI-powered learning experiences for over 3,000 customers worldwide. We have successfully achieved two IPOs (TSX: DCBO & NASDAQ: DCBO), been recognized as a top SaaS e-learning solution, and are growing exponentially in the process. We're a global company, with office across North America, EMEA, APAC, and beyond. Our team is guided by five core values—Grow Together Win Together, Build with Our Customer, Clear is Kind, Own Outcomes, Progress Over Perfection—that shape everything we do. If this resonates with you, now is the perfect time to join one of the fastest-growing learning technology companies in the world.

Docebo is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We are committed to diversity and inclusion in our workforce. All qualified applicants and employees will receive consideration for employment regardless of their race, colour, religion, sex (including pregnancy, gender identity, and sexual orientation), national origin, citizenship status, age, disability, genetic information, or any other category protected under applicable law.

As a federal contractor, Docebo is committed to the principles of affirmative action and equal employment opportunity for protected veterans and individuals with disabilities. Docebo does not discriminate because of protected veteran status or on the basis of disability, and Docebo takes affirmative action to employ and advance in employment qualified protected veterans and individuals with disabilities.

Any individuals requiring a reasonable accommodation or would like to voluntarily disclose a disability or protected veteran status to assist with their employment application should send an e-mail to recruiting_accommodations@docebo.com. The email should also include the position you’re interested in.

Compensation Range: CA$54.1K - CA$72.1K

Title: Analyst - Safety Data Programs
Description:
This position requires in-person collaboration up to five days a week. This arrangement is subject to change based on business requirements.

Why WestJet

Since our story took off in 1996, WestJet has strived to enrich the lives of everyone in our world; a career with us is no exception. We’re proud to be one of Canada’s most admired and respected corporate cultures and continuously aim for the highest standards of service and success.

Join us, this is where your career story takes off.

Overview Of The Role

WestJet is seeking an Analyst, Safety Data Programs. Reporting to the Senior Manager, Group Audit, Investigations & Safety Data, this role is responsible for developing, maintaining, and advancing analytics and statistical models focused on Safety and Flight Data Monitoring (FDM).

Leveraging data from the Company Reporting System and multiple internal sources, including WestJet aircraft systems, you will generate insights that support stakeholders in achieving safety objectives. You will also contribute to broader initiatives by producing reports, ensuring data integrity, and applying advanced analytics across large, complex datasets.

This role sits within a fast-paced, high-visibility team that operates with a strong commitment to accuracy, service excellence, and delivering high-quality insights to stakeholders and senior leadership.

In support of the FDM program, you will collaborate closely with Air Line Pilots Association-designated gatekeepers, flight safety teams, and safety investigators to support and strengthen WestJet’s FDM program, ultimately contributing to overall operational safety and performance.

Key Deliverables Include


Review, analyze, and combine data from multiple sources
Perform data quality assessments and in-depth analysis of datasets
Troubleshoot data and data analysis issues
Create, maintain, and update 150+ Power BI reports
Use statistical tools and methodology to identify, analyze, and interpret patterns and trends
Write advanced SQL queries to analyze, store, sort, and retrieve data for further analysis
Extract and communicate insights from raw data
Handle day-to-day data requests
Grow the program to use more in-depth and complex statistical tools, including, but not limited to, machine learning, simulations, predictive modelling, and reliable statistical methodologies
Meet with stakeholders to discern goals, objectives, and KPIs relating to data systems controlled by the team, and provide meaningful reports and insights related to those goals
Analytical mind with strong problem-solving skills


Experience And Qualifications


3 years+ of BI Analytics development experience, or a Bachelor's or master’s degree in mathematics, statistics, engineering, operations research, data science, or a related field.
Practical knowledge of data modelling, architecture, and warehousing
Practical knowledge of SQL
Expert knowledge of Microsoft Excel


The Following Would Be An Asset


Previous experience with flight operations and/or Flight Data Monitoring (FDM) analysis and software
Intermediate to advanced DAX, PowerShell, or VBA
Previous experience with advanced statistical modeling
Previous experience with safety reporting or safety management systems
Knowledge of aircraft systems and performance, preferably Boeing 737/787 and Q400 aircraft


The Benefits Of Being a WestJetter

WestJet provides all WestJetters with a competitive total rewards package. On top of that, we offer:


A fun and friendly culture with colleagues who work together to win
Travel privileges for you and your family, effective from your start date
Savings and Benefit programs that are flexible to meet your specific needs


Think we’re a great fit? Apply now!

About WestJet Group Of Companies

WestJet took to the skies in 1996 with just over 200 employees and three aircraft operating service to five destinations. Since then, WestJet has pioneered low-cost travel in Canada, cutting airfares in half, and increasing the flying population in Canada by more than 50 per cent. Since the Sunwing Airlines integration in 2025, more than 15,000 WestJetters support nearly 200 aircraft and connect guests to our global network of more than 100 destinations across North America, Central America, the Caribbean, Europe and Asia. The WestJet Group is a major Canadian employer and includes WestJet Airlines, WestJet Encore and Sunwing Vacations Group. We’re Canada’s leading value airline and largest vacation provider, with a united purpose of providing affordable and accessible air and vacation travel to Canadians.

Our safety promise

At WestJet, the safety and security of our people and our guests is a core value and at the heart of what we do. Safety and security are shared responsibilities all team members are expected to use safe work practices to ensure their well-being and the safety of others. WestJet recognizes that the use of Alcohol and Drugs can adversely impact a safe work environment and the well-being of others including guests, suppliers and the public, as well as place WestJet's operations at risk. All roles that are identified as Safey-Sensitive are required to pass a Pre-Employment Alcohol and Drug Test as per WestJet's Alcohol and Drug Policy. 

Our commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion

We embrace what makes each of us unique, and what makes us uniquely WestJet. We welcome diverse skills, experiences and backgrounds so if you're passionate about this role, we encourage you to apply – even if you don't meet every qualification listed. WestJet is committed to inclusiveness, equity and accessibility throughout the selection process. If you require accommodation at any stage, please contact our Talent Acquisition team at talent@westjet.com.

We value the time and effort you put into your application. Our recruitment process is highly competitive and while every applicant will receive communication regarding their application status, only those selected to move forward will be contacted with next steps. If this role isn’t the right fit, we encourage you to explore future opportunities with us.

About

For more information about everything WestJet, please westjet.com/aboutus.

If you wish to withdraw your application or request that your personal information be deleted, please contact us at talent@westjet.com.

Title: Advanced Data Analyst - EA Sports FC Live
Description:
Description & Requirements

Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.

Advanced Data Analyst - FC Live Service Forecasting

Our FC Data & Analytics team is looking for an Advanced Analyst to join our group, leading forecasting and performance modelling for FC’s live service business. Reporting to a Senior Analytics Manager, you will be part of a growing team providing data and insights around player behaviour and live content performance within the FC franchise. We’re looking for someone who combines strong quantitative forecasting expertise with business intuition, bringing the ability to translate historical trends, player behaviour, and live content strategies into actionable projections that inform decision-making. This role sits at the intersection of analytics, strategy, and live service operations - helping shape how FC plans and measures performance at scale.

What You'll Do On Our Team


Own the forecasting framework that underpins performance measurement, planning, and strategic decision-making across FC’s live service business.
Employ an extensive toolkit of financial modelling techniques, time series analysis, and a deep knowledge of their use cases.
Develop a robust model that projects our live service funnel metrics to a high degree of fidelity, incorporating player behaviour, in-game content, and macroeconomic trends.
Collaborate with partners across Product Management, Finance, and Commercial teams to align on projections, and keep the business updated on how our latest live service performance impacts our model’s assumptions.
Establish scalable forecasting processes that enable us to work with our cross-functional partners to efficiently & effectively refine our projections over time.
Lead presentations to diverse audiences including senior and executive level management, distilling complex model results into clear insights & recommendations.
Run risk and opportunity scenarios based on live content plans, new feature additions, and other potential changes in the FC product.
Mentor and collaborate with other analysts in order to collaboratively develop our forecasts, working together to drive evolutions in our modelling in this space.


What We're Looking For


A degree in a quantitative discipline (Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Finance, or similar)
7+ years of experience in a data-driven role performing quantitative analysis with big data to help guide decisions
4+ years of experience developing financial/product performance projections and forecasts using various modelling techniques
Strong experience in statistical and/or time series modelling for predictive purposes
Expertise in SQL and working with large-scale, complex datasets
Proficient in analyzing large datasets using programmatic tools (e.g. Python, R)
Experience with data visualization software (e.g. Tableau, PowerBI)
Strong capabilities in project management processes
Very comfortable presenting analysis in an executive facing environment


Pay Transparency - North America

Compensation And Benefits

The ranges listed below are what EA in good faith expects to pay applicants for this role in these locations at the time of this posting. If you reside in a different location, a recruiter will advise on the applicable range and benefits. Pay offered will be determined based on a number of relevant business and candidate factors (e.g. education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, or business needs).

PAY RANGES


British Columbia (depending on location e.g. Vancouver vs. Victoria) *$114,300 - $156,200 CAD


Pay is just one part of the overall compensation at EA.

For Canada, we offer a package of benefits including vacation (3 weeks per year to start), 10 days per year of sick time, paid top-up to EI/QPIP benefits up to 100% of base salary when you welcome a new child (12 weeks for maternity, and 4 weeks for parental/adoption leave), extended health/dental/vision coverage, life insurance, disability insurance, retirement plan to regular full-time employees. Certain roles may also be eligible for bonus and equity.

We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
JD:

Title: Data analyst

Location: Dieppe, New Brunswick (4 days onsite)

Duration: 12 months




Key Responsibilities

Low-Code Automation & Technology Enablement

Design, build, and support simple business solutions using Power Apps and Power Automate.
Identify manual processes and develop automation opportunities to improve efficiency and reduce operational risk.
Create and enhance workflows to support business processes, approvals, data collection, and reporting activities.
Support maintenance, enhancements, and issue resolution for existing Power Platform solutions.
Collaborate with business users to understand needs and deliver practical automation solutions.
Evaluate opportunities to use low-code technologies to improve user experience and streamline operations.

Process Improvement & Optimization

Analyze existing business processes and identify opportunities for simplification, standardization, and automation.
Document current-state and future-state workflows.
Perform root cause analysis to identify process inefficiencies, pain points, and operational risks.
Recommend and support implementation of process improvements.
Contribute to continuous improvement and transformation initiatives.

Data & Analytics

Analyze business and operational data to identify trends, risks, exceptions, and opportunities.
Design and develop reports, dashboards, and visualizations using Power BI and related tools.
Define, document, and track business KPIs and performance metrics.
Translate business questions into meaningful metrics and actionable insights.
Partner with stakeholders to understand reporting and analytical requirements.

Data Management & Design

Understand and document data flows across business processes and systems.
Support data mapping, data flow documentation, and reporting requirements.
Assist with basic data modelling and reporting design activities.
Help ensure business requirements are aligned with reporting, analytics, and data solutions.




Required Qualifications

2-4 years of experience in one or more of the following areas: process improvement, data and analytics, reporting and dashboard development, Power Platform development, business operations, or low-code automation.
Hands-on experience building or supporting solutions using Power Apps and/or Power Automate.
Experience working with data to support business decision-making.
Experience developing reports, dashboards, KPIs, performance metrics, or analytical insights.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and process-thinking skills.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
Ability to work effectively across business and technical teams in a collaborative environment.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and adapt to changing business needs.




Preferred Qualifications

Power BI development and dashboard design experience.
Experience with SharePoint, Dataverse, Microsoft Lists, or related Microsoft 365 technologies.
Understanding of data flows, data mapping, and basic data modelling concepts.
Exposure to Lean, Six Sigma, or other process improvement methodologies.
Experience documenting processes, workflows, business rules, or operational procedures.
Exposure to VBA, Python, or Alteryx.
Financial services experience is considered an asset.




Highest Priority Skills: Power Apps, Power Automate, process improvement, Power BI/reporting, stakeholder communication

Strongly Preferred Skills: Data flow analysis, KPI/dashboard development, SharePoint/Dataverse/Microsoft Lists, basic data modelling

Nice-to-Have Skills: VBA, Python, Alteryx, Lean/Six Sigma, financial services experience

As one of the largest IT staffing companies and the 2nd largest minority-owned staffing company in the U.S., Dexian was formed in 2023 through the merger of DISYS and Signature Consultants. Combining the best elements of its core companies, Dexian's platform connects talent, technology, and organizations to produce game-changing results that help everyone achieve their ambitions and goals.

Dexian's brands include Dexian DISYS, Dexian Signature Consultants, Dexian Government Solutions, Dexian Talent Development and Dexian IT Solutions. Visit https://dexian.com/ to learn more.
